**Output:**
```
{
  "kind": "success",
  "message": "Doc intelligence action completed",
  "data": {
    "action": "chunk",
    "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
    "chunks": [
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 1,
        "heading": "Advanced Repomix + Opencode Orchestration: Deep Research & Cross-Dependency Cheat Sheet",
        "startLine": 8,
        "endLine": 11,
        "content": "# Advanced Repomix + Opencode Orchestration: Deep Research & Cross-Dependency Cheat Sheet\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 94,
        "estimatedTokens": 24,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#advanced-repomix-opencode-orchestration-deep-research-cross-dependency-cheat-sheet-1"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 2,
        "heading": "Part I: Opencode Tool Taxonomy -- What Agents Underutilize",
        "startLine": 12,
        "endLine": 13,
        "content": "## Part I: Opencode Tool Taxonomy -- What Agents Underutilize",
        "characterCount": 61,
        "estimatedTokens": 16,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#part-i-opencode-tool-taxonomy-what-agents-underutilize-2"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 3,
        "heading": "1.1 Complete Tool Registry",
        "startLine": 14,
        "endLine": 21,
        "content": "### 1.1 Complete Tool Registry\n\nOpencode registers tools in `ToolRegistry` with this priority order: [1-cite-0](#1-cite-0) \n\n| Tool | Kind | What agents miss | Key params |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `read` | read | **Offset reading** for large files, directory listing mode | `filePath`, `offset` (1-indexed), `limit` (default 2000) |\n| `grep` | search | `include` glob filter, results sorted by mtime | `pattern` (regex), `path`, `include` |",
        "characterCount": 436,
        "estimatedTokens": 109,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#11-complete-tool-registry-3"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 4,
        "heading": "1.1 Complete Tool Registry",
        "startLine": 22,
        "endLine": 26,
        "content": "| `glob` | search | Results sorted by mtime (most recent first), 100-file limit | `pattern`, `path` |\n| `list` | read | Accepts glob patterns for filtering | `path` |\n| `bash` | execute | Full shell -- git, curl, jq, sed, awk, piping | `command`, `description` |\n| `webfetch` | fetch | `format`: text/markdown/html, timeout control | `url`, `format`, `timeout` |\n| `websearch` | search | `type`: auto/fast/deep, `livecrawl`: fallback/preferred | `query`, `numResults`, `type` |",
        "characterCount": 477,
        "estimatedTokens": 120,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#11-complete-tool-registry-4"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 5,
        "heading": "1.1 Complete Tool Registry",
        "startLine": 27,
        "endLine": 31,
        "content": "| `codesearch` | search | **Exa Code API** for npm/library docs, 1K-50K tokens | `query`, `tokensNum` |\n| `lsp` | other | **9 operations** -- experimental, needs env flag | `operation`, `filePath`, `line`, `character` |\n| `skill` | other | Loads `SKILL.md` + bundled files into context | `name` |\n| `task` | other | Subagent delegation with `task_id` resume | `prompt`, `description`, `subagent_type`, `task_id` |\n| `batch` | other | **Parallel tool execution**, 1-25 calls | `tool_calls[]` |",
        "characterCount": 492,
        "estimatedTokens": 123,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#11-complete-tool-registry-5"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 6,
        "heading": "1.1 Complete Tool Registry",
        "startLine": 32,
        "endLine": 36,
        "content": "| `apply_patch` | edit | Multi-file atomic patches with LSP diagnostics | `patchText` |\n| `edit` | edit | Surgical line edits | `filePath`, ... |\n| `write` | edit | Create/overwrite files | `filePath`, `content` |\n| `todowrite` | other | Persistent task tracking across turns | `todos[]` |",
        "characterCount": 289,
        "estimatedTokens": 73,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#11-complete-tool-registry-6"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 7,
        "heading": "1.2 The Read Tool -- Offset Reading (Agents Almost Never Use This)",
        "startLine": 37,
        "endLine": 47,
        "content": "### 1.2 The Read Tool -- Offset Reading (Agents Almost Never Use This)\n\nThe `ReadTool` has a 50KB byte cap and 2000-line default limit. When truncated, it explicitly tells the agent to use `offset`: [1-cite-1](#1-cite-1) \n\n```\nOutput capped at 50 KB. Showing lines 1-847. Use offset=848 to continue.\nShowing lines 1-2000 of 5432. Use offset=2001 to continue.\n``` [1-cite-2](#1-cite-2) \n\n**Orchestrator instruction pattern:**\n```",
        "characterCount": 428,
        "estimatedTokens": 107,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#12-the-read-tool-offset-reading-agents-almost-never-use-this-7"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 8,
        "heading": "1.2 The Read Tool -- Offset Reading (Agents Almost Never Use This)",
        "startLine": 48,
        "endLine": 52,
        "content": "Read the file at /path/to/large-file.ts. If truncated, continue reading \nwith offset= as indicated until you have the complete picture of [specific \nsection/function/class]. Report back the full content of [target].\n```",
        "characterCount": 219,
        "estimatedTokens": 55,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#12-the-read-tool-offset-reading-agents-almost-never-use-this-8"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 9,
        "heading": "1.3 LSP Tool -- The Most Underused Power Tool",
        "startLine": 53,
        "endLine": 64,
        "content": "### 1.3 LSP Tool -- The Most Underused Power Tool\n\nRequires `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TOOL=true` (or `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL=true`). [1-cite-3](#1-cite-3) \n\n9 operations available:\n\n| Operation | Use Case in Research |\n|---|---|\n| `goToDefinition` | Trace where a type/function is actually defined |\n| `findReferences` | Find all callers/consumers of a symbol |\n| `hover` | Get type signature without reading full file |\n| `documentSymbol` | List all symbols in a file (classes, functions, exports) |",
        "characterCount": 500,
        "estimatedTokens": 125,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#13-lsp-tool-the-most-underused-power-tool-9"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 10,
        "heading": "1.3 LSP Tool -- The Most Underused Power Tool",
        "startLine": 65,
        "endLine": 73,
        "content": "| `workspaceSymbol` | Search symbols across entire workspace |\n| `goToImplementation` | Find concrete implementations of interfaces |\n| `prepareCallHierarchy` | Set up call hierarchy analysis |\n| `incomingCalls` | Who calls this function? |\n| `outgoingCalls` | What does this function call? | [1-cite-4](#1-cite-4) \n\n**Orchestrator instruction pattern:**\n```\nUse the LSP tool to trace the call hierarchy of `delegateTask` in",
        "characterCount": 424,
        "estimatedTokens": 106,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#13-lsp-tool-the-most-underused-power-tool-10"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 11,
        "heading": "1.3 LSP Tool -- The Most Underused Power Tool",
        "startLine": 74,
        "endLine": 78,
        "content": "src/delegation/manager.ts. First use documentSymbol to find the line number, \nthen use incomingCalls and outgoingCalls to map the full call graph. \nReport the dependency chain.\n```",
        "characterCount": 180,
        "estimatedTokens": 45,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#13-lsp-tool-the-most-underused-power-tool-11"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 12,
        "heading": "1.4 CodeSearch -- npm/Library Documentation on Demand",
        "startLine": 79,
        "endLine": 86,
        "content": "### 1.4 CodeSearch -- npm/Library Documentation on Demand\n\nUses Exa Code API (`get_code_context_exa`) -- no API key needed. Returns code examples, docs, and API references for any library/SDK. [1-cite-5](#1-cite-5) \n\n**Key insight agents miss:** `tokensNum` is adjustable from 1,000 to 50,000. Default 5,000 is often too low for comprehensive library research.\n\n```\ncodesearch({ query: \"zod schema validation advanced patterns discriminated unions\", tokensNum: 20000 })",
        "characterCount": 469,
        "estimatedTokens": 118,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#14-codesearch-npmlibrary-documentation-on-demand-12"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 13,
        "heading": "1.4 CodeSearch -- npm/Library Documentation on Demand",
        "startLine": 87,
        "endLine": 90,
        "content": "codesearch({ query: \"Model Context Protocol SDK server tool registration TypeScript\", tokensNum: 15000 })\ncodesearch({ query: \"Tree-sitter TypeScript parser AST node types\", tokensNum: 10000 })\n```",
        "characterCount": 197,
        "estimatedTokens": 50,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#14-codesearch-npmlibrary-documentation-on-demand-13"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 14,
        "heading": "1.5 WebSearch vs WebFetch -- Discovery vs Retrieval [1-cite-6](#1-cite-6) [1-cite-7](#1-cite-7)",
        "startLine": 91,
        "endLine": 98,
        "content": "### 1.5 WebSearch vs WebFetch -- Discovery vs Retrieval [1-cite-6](#1-cite-6) [1-cite-7](#1-cite-7) \n\n| | `websearch` | `webfetch` |\n|---|---|---|\n| Purpose | **Discovery** -- find what exists | **Retrieval** -- get specific content |\n| Backend | Exa AI MCP (`web_search_exa`) | Direct HTTP fetch |\n| Auth | None needed | None needed |\n| Enable | `OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA=1` or OpenCode provider | Always available |",
        "characterCount": 412,
        "estimatedTokens": 103,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#15-websearch-vs-webfetch-discovery-vs-retrieval-1-cite-61-cite-6-1-cite-71-cite-7-14"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 15,
        "heading": "1.5 WebSearch vs WebFetch -- Discovery vs Retrieval [1-cite-6](#1-cite-6) [1-cite-7](#1-cite-7)",
        "startLine": 99,
        "endLine": 107,
        "content": "| Key params | `query`, `type` (auto/fast/deep), `numResults` | `url`, `format` (text/markdown/html) |\n\n**Research chain pattern:**\n```\n1. websearch({ query: \"hivemind plugin architecture patterns 2025\", type: \"deep\" })\n2. webfetch({ url: \"<best result URL>\", format: \"markdown\" })\n3. codesearch({ query: \"<specific API from the article>\", tokensNum: 15000 })\n```",
        "characterCount": 363,
        "estimatedTokens": 91,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#15-websearch-vs-webfetch-discovery-vs-retrieval-1-cite-61-cite-6-1-cite-71-cite-7-15"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 16,
        "heading": "1.6 Context7 MCP -- Library Documentation Search",
        "startLine": 108,
        "endLine": 130,
        "content": "### 1.6 Context7 MCP -- Library Documentation Search\n\nOpencode recognizes `context7_resolve_library_id` and `context7_get_library_docs` as search-kind tools. [1-cite-8](#1-cite-8) \n\nConfigure in `opencode.json`:\n```json\n{\n  \"mcp\": {\n    \"context7\": {\n      \"type\": \"remote\",\n      \"url\": \"https://mcp.context7.com/mcp\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n``` [1-cite-9](#1-cite-9) \n\n**Usage pattern:** Add `use context7` to prompts, or put in `AGENTS.md`:\n```md\nWhen you need to search docs, use `context7` tools.\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 498,
        "estimatedTokens": 125,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#16-context7-mcp-library-documentation-search-16"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 17,
        "heading": "Part II: Orchestrator & Swarm Delegation Patterns",
        "startLine": 131,
        "endLine": 132,
        "content": "## Part II: Orchestrator & Swarm Delegation Patterns",
        "characterCount": 52,
        "estimatedTokens": 13,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#part-ii-orchestrator-swarm-delegation-patterns-17"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 18,
        "heading": "2.1 Agent Architecture in Opencode",
        "startLine": 133,
        "endLine": 141,
        "content": "### 2.1 Agent Architecture in Opencode\n\nOpencode has 4 native visible agents + hidden utility agents: [1-cite-10](#1-cite-10) \n\n| Agent | Mode | Tools | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `build` | primary | All (with question, plan_enter) | Default coding agent |\n| `plan` | primary | Read-only + plan files | Planning mode, no edits |\n| `general` | subagent | All except todo | Multi-step autonomous tasks |",
        "characterCount": 404,
        "estimatedTokens": 101,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#21-agent-architecture-in-opencode-18"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 19,
        "heading": "2.1 Agent Architecture in Opencode",
        "startLine": 142,
        "endLine": 148,
        "content": "| `explore` | subagent | grep, glob, list, bash, read, webfetch, websearch, codesearch | **Read-only codebase exploration** |\n| `compaction` | primary (hidden) | None | Context compaction |\n| `title` | primary (hidden) | None | Title generation |\n| `summary` | primary (hidden) | None | Summary generation |\n\nThe `explore` agent is the key research subagent -- it has read-only permissions and access to all search/fetch tools: [1-cite-11](#1-cite-11)",
        "characterCount": 451,
        "estimatedTokens": 113,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#21-agent-architecture-in-opencode-19"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 20,
        "heading": "2.2 Task Tool -- Subagent Delegation",
        "startLine": 149,
        "endLine": 159,
        "content": "### 2.2 Task Tool -- Subagent Delegation\n\nThe `TaskTool` is how orchestrators spawn subagents: [1-cite-12](#1-cite-12) \n\nCritical patterns agents miss:\n\n**a) Concurrent subagent launch** (single message, multiple tool uses):\n```\n// Launch 3 explore agents in parallel\nTask(description=\"Map hivemind-plugin architecture\", prompt=\"...\", subagent_type=\"explore\")\nTask(description=\"Map openagent MCP layer\", prompt=\"...\", subagent_type=\"explore\")",
        "characterCount": 442,
        "estimatedTokens": 111,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#22-task-tool-subagent-delegation-20"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 21,
        "heading": "2.2 Task Tool -- Subagent Delegation",
        "startLine": 160,
        "endLine": 171,
        "content": "Task(description=\"Map opencode SDK plugin system\", prompt=\"...\", subagent_type=\"explore\")\n``` [1-cite-13](#1-cite-13) \n\n**b) Session resumption** via `task_id`:\n```\nTask(task_id=\"ses_abc123\", prompt=\"Continue from where you left off. Now trace the delegation chain...\", subagent_type=\"explore\")\n``` [1-cite-14](#1-cite-14) \n\n**c) Research vs. code distinction** -- always tell the subagent:\n```\nTask(\n  description=\"Research hivemind delegation layer\",",
        "characterCount": 452,
        "estimatedTokens": 113,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#22-task-tool-subagent-delegation-21"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 22,
        "heading": "2.2 Task Tool -- Subagent Delegation",
        "startLine": 172,
        "endLine": 181,
        "content": "prompt=\"This is a RESEARCH-ONLY task. Do NOT modify any files. \n  Use grep, glob, read, and bash (git log only) to:\n  1. Find all files in src/delegation/\n  2. Map the class hierarchy\n  3. Identify all public APIs\n  Return a structured summary with file paths and key function signatures.\",\n  subagent_type=\"explore\"\n)\n``` [1-cite-15](#1-cite-15)",
        "characterCount": 346,
        "estimatedTokens": 87,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#22-task-tool-subagent-delegation-22"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 23,
        "heading": "2.3 Task Permission Control for Orchestrator Patterns",
        "startLine": 182,
        "endLine": 201,
        "content": "### 2.3 Task Permission Control for Orchestrator Patterns\n\nConfigure which subagents an orchestrator can invoke:\n```json\n{\n  \"agent\": {\n    \"orchestrator\": {\n      \"mode\": \"primary\",\n      \"permission\": {\n        \"task\": {\n          \"*\": \"ask\",\n          \"explore\": \"allow\",\n          \"general\": \"allow\",\n          \"researcher-*\": \"allow\"\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```",
        "characterCount": 373,
        "estimatedTokens": 94,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#23-task-permission-control-for-orchestrator-patterns-23"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 24,
        "heading": "2.3 Task Permission Control for Orchestrator Patterns",
        "startLine": 202,
        "endLine": 203,
        "content": "Last matching rule wins. When set to `ask`, the subagent is removed from the Task tool description entirely. [1-cite-16](#1-cite-16)",
        "characterCount": 133,
        "estimatedTokens": 34,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#23-task-permission-control-for-orchestrator-patterns-24"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 25,
        "heading": "2.4 Batch Tool -- Parallel Tool Execution Within a Single Agent",
        "startLine": 204,
        "endLine": 212,
        "content": "### 2.4 Batch Tool -- Parallel Tool Execution Within a Single Agent\n\nThe `BatchTool` executes 1-25 tool calls concurrently. This is the **intra-agent parallelism** complement to Task's **inter-agent parallelism**. [1-cite-17](#1-cite-17) [1-cite-18](#1-cite-18) \n\nEnable with `experimental.batch_tool: true` in config.\n\n```json\n// Batch payload example: read 3 files + grep 2 patterns simultaneously\n[",
        "characterCount": 401,
        "estimatedTokens": 101,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#24-batch-tool-parallel-tool-execution-within-a-single-agent-25"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 26,
        "heading": "2.4 Batch Tool -- Parallel Tool Execution Within a Single Agent",
        "startLine": 213,
        "endLine": 216,
        "content": "{\"tool\": \"read\", \"parameters\": {\"filePath\": \"/path/to/hivemind/src/core/index.ts\", \"limit\": 500}},\n  {\"tool\": \"read\", \"parameters\": {\"filePath\": \"/path/to/hivemind/src/delegation/manager.ts\", \"limit\": 500}},\n  {\"tool\": \"read\", \"parameters\": {\"filePath\": \"/path/to/hivemind/src/intelligence/engine.ts\", \"limit\": 500}},\n  {\"tool\": \"grep\", \"parameters\": {\"pattern\": \"export class.*Plugin\", \"path\": \"/path/to/hivemind/src\"}},",
        "characterCount": 421,
        "estimatedTokens": 106,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#24-batch-tool-parallel-tool-execution-within-a-single-agent-26"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 27,
        "heading": "2.4 Batch Tool -- Parallel Tool Execution Within a Single Agent",
        "startLine": 217,
        "endLine": 220,
        "content": "{\"tool\": \"grep\", \"parameters\": {\"pattern\": \"implements.*Interface\", \"path\": \"/path/to/hivemind/src\"}}\n]\n```",
        "characterCount": 107,
        "estimatedTokens": 27,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#24-batch-tool-parallel-tool-execution-within-a-single-agent-27"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 28,
        "heading": "2.5 Apply Patch -- Iterative Document Updates",
        "startLine": 221,
        "endLine": 231,
        "content": "### 2.5 Apply Patch -- Iterative Document Updates\n\nThe `ApplyPatchTool` supports multi-file atomic patches with add/update/delete/move operations and automatic LSP diagnostics after application: [1-cite-19](#1-cite-19) [1-cite-20](#1-cite-20) \n\n**For iterative synthesis documents:**\n```\napply_patch({\n  patchText: `*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: .opencode/research/hivemind-architecture.md\n@@@ --- a/.opencode/research/hivemind-architecture.md\n+++ b/.opencode/research/hivemind-architecture.md",
        "characterCount": 496,
        "estimatedTokens": 124,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#25-apply-patch-iterative-document-updates-28"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 29,
        "heading": "2.5 Apply Patch -- Iterative Document Updates",
        "startLine": 232,
        "endLine": 245,
        "content": "@@ Section: Delegation Layer @@\n-TODO: Map delegation patterns\n+## Delegation Layer\n+\n+### Core Classes\n+- DelegationManager (src/delegation/manager.ts:45)\n+- TaskRouter (src/delegation/router.ts:12)\n+...\n*** End Patch`\n})\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 231,
        "estimatedTokens": 58,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#25-apply-patch-iterative-document-updates-29"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 30,
        "heading": "Part III: Repomix Advanced Techniques for Deep Research",
        "startLine": 246,
        "endLine": 247,
        "content": "## Part III: Repomix Advanced Techniques for Deep Research",
        "characterCount": 58,
        "estimatedTokens": 15,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#part-iii-repomix-advanced-techniques-for-deep-research-30"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 31,
        "heading": "3.1 Remote Repository Packing for Cross-Dependency Research",
        "startLine": 248,
        "endLine": 259,
        "content": "### 3.1 Remote Repository Packing for Cross-Dependency Research\n\nPack any GitHub repo (public or accessible) without cloning locally: [1-cite-21](#1-cite-21) \n\n```jsonc\n// Pack a specific npm library's source to understand its internals\n{ \"remote\": \"yamadashy/repomix\", \"includePatterns\": \"src/**/*.ts\", \"compress\": true }\n\n// Pack a dependency you're investigating\n{ \"remote\": \"modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk\", \"includePatterns\": \"src/**\" }\n\n// Pack with branch targeting",
        "characterCount": 477,
        "estimatedTokens": 120,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#31-remote-repository-packing-for-cross-dependency-research-31"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 32,
        "heading": "3.1 Remote Repository Packing for Cross-Dependency Research",
        "startLine": 260,
        "endLine": 263,
        "content": "{ \"remote\": \"https://github.com/shynlee04/hivemind-plugin/tree/v2.9.5-detox-dev\",\n  \"includePatterns\": \"src/delegation/**,src/intelligence/**\" }\n```",
        "characterCount": 148,
        "estimatedTokens": 37,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#31-remote-repository-packing-for-cross-dependency-research-32"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 33,
        "heading": "3.2 Skill Generation as Persistent Knowledge Artifacts",
        "startLine": 264,
        "endLine": 275,
        "content": "### 3.2 Skill Generation as Persistent Knowledge Artifacts\n\nThe `generate_skill` tool creates a structured knowledge package that persists across sessions: [1-cite-22](#1-cite-22) \n\n**Output structure:**\n```\n.claude/skills/<skill-name>/\n├── SKILL.md                    # Entry point with usage guide\n└── references/\n    ├── summary.md              # Purpose, format, and statistics\n    ├── project-structure.md    # Directory tree with line counts\n    ├── files.md                # All file contents",
        "characterCount": 499,
        "estimatedTokens": 125,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#32-skill-generation-as-persistent-knowledge-artifacts-33"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 34,
        "heading": "3.2 Skill Generation as Persistent Knowledge Artifacts",
        "startLine": 276,
        "endLine": 281,
        "content": "└── tech-stacks.md          # Languages, frameworks, dependencies\n```\n\n**The skill loading chain in opencode:**\nWhen an agent calls `skill({ name: \"...\" })`, the `SkillTool` loads `SKILL.md` content + up to 10 bundled files into the conversation context: [1-cite-23](#1-cite-23)",
        "characterCount": 278,
        "estimatedTokens": 70,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#32-skill-generation-as-persistent-knowledge-artifacts-34"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 35,
        "heading": "3.3 The Pack-Grep-Read Pipeline (Incremental Exploration)",
        "startLine": 282,
        "endLine": 290,
        "content": "### 3.3 The Pack-Grep-Read Pipeline (Incremental Exploration)\n\nThis is the core pattern for token-efficient deep research. The repomix MCP server instructions explicitly describe this workflow: [1-cite-24](#1-cite-24) \n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    A[\"pack_codebase / pack_remote_repository\\n(get outputId + directory structure)\"] --> B[\"grep_repomix_output\\n(regex search within packed output)\"]\n    B --> C[\"read_repomix_output\\n(read specific line ranges)\"]\n    C --> D{\"Need more detail?\"}",
        "characterCount": 492,
        "estimatedTokens": 123,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#33-the-pack-grep-read-pipeline-incremental-exploration-35"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 36,
        "heading": "3.3 The Pack-Grep-Read Pipeline (Incremental Exploration)",
        "startLine": 291,
        "endLine": 301,
        "content": "D -->|\"Yes\"| B\n    D -->|\"No\"| E[\"Synthesize findings\\ninto skill or document\"]\n```\n\n**grep_repomix_output** supports asymmetric context windows: [1-cite-25](#1-cite-25) \n\n```jsonc\n// Show 2 lines before, 15 lines after each match (see full function body after signature)\n{ \"outputId\": \"<id>\", \"pattern\": \"export class DelegationManager\", \"beforeLines\": 2, \"afterLines\": 15 }\n\n// Case-insensitive search for cross-cutting concerns",
        "characterCount": 430,
        "estimatedTokens": 108,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#33-the-pack-grep-read-pipeline-incremental-exploration-36"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 37,
        "heading": "3.3 The Pack-Grep-Read Pipeline (Incremental Exploration)",
        "startLine": 302,
        "endLine": 304,
        "content": "{ \"outputId\": \"<id>\", \"pattern\": \"lifecycle|dispose|cleanup|teardown\", \"ignoreCase\": true, \"contextLines\": 3 }\n```",
        "characterCount": 114,
        "estimatedTokens": 29,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#33-the-pack-grep-read-pipeline-incremental-exploration-37"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 38,
        "heading": "3.4 Stacking Skills for Multi-Repo Synthesis",
        "startLine": 305,
        "endLine": 316,
        "content": "### 3.4 Stacking Skills for Multi-Repo Synthesis\n\n**Phase 1: Generate skills from each repo**\n```jsonc\n// Via repomix MCP\ngenerate_skill({ directory: \"/path/to/hivemind-plugin\", skillName: \"hivemind-core\", compress: true,\n  includePatterns: \"src/**/*.ts\", ignorePatterns: \"**/*.test.*,**/*.spec.*\" })\n\ngenerate_skill({ directory: \"/path/to/oh-my-openagent\", skillName: \"openagent-mcp-system\",\n  includePatterns: \"src/mcp/**,src/plugin/**,src/hooks/**\" })\n\n// For remote repos you don't have locally:",
        "characterCount": 499,
        "estimatedTokens": 125,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#34-stacking-skills-for-multi-repo-synthesis-38"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 39,
        "heading": "3.4 Stacking Skills for Multi-Repo Synthesis",
        "startLine": 317,
        "endLine": 330,
        "content": "// First pack_remote_repository, then use the output as reference\n```\n\n**Phase 2: Load skills in orchestrator context**\n```\nskill({ name: \"hivemind-core\" })\nskill({ name: \"openagent-mcp-system\" })\n// Now the agent has both codebases in context for cross-reference\n```\n\n**Phase 3: Cross-reference with targeted searches**\n```\n// Use opencode grep to find live code patterns\ngrep({ pattern: \"McpServer|registerTool|MCP\", path: \"/path/to/hivemind-plugin/src\" })",
        "characterCount": 458,
        "estimatedTokens": 115,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#34-stacking-skills-for-multi-repo-synthesis-39"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 40,
        "heading": "3.4 Stacking Skills for Multi-Repo Synthesis",
        "startLine": 331,
        "endLine": 336,
        "content": "// Use repomix grep on packed openagent output\ngrep_repomix_output({ outputId: \"<openagent-id>\", pattern: \"McpServer|registerTool\" })\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 142,
        "estimatedTokens": 36,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#34-stacking-skills-for-multi-repo-synthesis-40"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 41,
        "heading": "Part IV: Orchestration Patterns Aligned with Your Research Protocol",
        "startLine": 337,
        "endLine": 338,
        "content": "## Part IV: Orchestration Patterns Aligned with Your Research Protocol",
        "characterCount": 70,
        "estimatedTokens": 18,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#part-iv-orchestration-patterns-aligned-with-your-research-protocol-41"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 42,
        "heading": "4.1 Mapping to Your Protocol's Core Constraints",
        "startLine": 339,
        "endLine": 345,
        "content": "### 4.1 Mapping to Your Protocol's Core Constraints\n\n| Protocol Rule | Implementation |\n|---|---|\n| No deep line-by-line reading | Use `grep` + `glob` first, then targeted `read` with `limit` |\n| No long-horizon agentic execution | Use `batch` for parallel tool calls; use `task` with specific bounded prompts |\n| No file mutation (research mode) | Use `explore` subagent (edit permissions denied); use `plan` mode |",
        "characterCount": 416,
        "estimatedTokens": 104,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#41-mapping-to-your-protocols-core-constraints-42"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 43,
        "heading": "4.1 Mapping to Your Protocol's Core Constraints",
        "startLine": 346,
        "endLine": 348,
        "content": "| Disk-based synthesis | Use `apply_patch` or `write` to create synthesis docs in `.opencode/research/` |\n| Batch planning for subagents | Launch multiple `task` calls in single message; chain via `task_id` |",
        "characterCount": 208,
        "estimatedTokens": 52,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#41-mapping-to-your-protocols-core-constraints-43"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 44,
        "heading": "4.2 The Hierarchical Exploration Pattern",
        "startLine": 349,
        "endLine": 361,
        "content": "### 4.2 The Hierarchical Exploration Pattern\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph \"Cycle 1: Frame\"\n        A1[\"Orchestrator: pack_codebase (compressed)\\nGet directory structure + metrics\"]\n        A2[\"Orchestrator: grep_repomix_output\\nfor entry points, exports, main\"]\n        A1 --> A2\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Cycle 2: Map (Parallel Subagents)\"\n        B1[\"explore: Map module A\\n(grep + glob + read)\"]\n        B2[\"explore: Map module B\\n(grep + glob + read)\"]",
        "characterCount": 462,
        "estimatedTokens": 116,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#42-the-hierarchical-exploration-pattern-44"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 45,
        "heading": "4.2 The Hierarchical Exploration Pattern",
        "startLine": 362,
        "endLine": 375,
        "content": "B3[\"explore: Map module C\\n(grep + glob + read)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Cycle 3: Trace (Sequential)\"\n        C1[\"explore: Trace cross-module deps\\n(LSP findReferences + incomingCalls)\"]\n        C2[\"explore: Trace external deps\\n(codesearch + webfetch for docs)\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Cycle 4: Synthesize\"\n        D1[\"Orchestrator: write synthesis doc\\n(.opencode/research/findings.md)\"]\n        D2[\"Orchestrator: generate_skill\\n(persist as reusable knowledge)\"]\n    end\n\n    A2 --> B1",
        "characterCount": 488,
        "estimatedTokens": 122,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#42-the-hierarchical-exploration-pattern-45"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 46,
        "heading": "4.2 The Hierarchical Exploration Pattern",
        "startLine": 376,
        "endLine": 385,
        "content": "A2 --> B2\n    A2 --> B3\n    B1 --> C1\n    B2 --> C1\n    B3 --> C1\n    C1 --> C2\n    C2 --> D1\n    D1 --> D2\n```",
        "characterCount": 111,
        "estimatedTokens": 28,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#42-the-hierarchical-exploration-pattern-46"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 47,
        "heading": "4.3 Concrete Orchestrator Prompt Templates",
        "startLine": 386,
        "endLine": 397,
        "content": "### 4.3 Concrete Orchestrator Prompt Templates\n\n**Template: Initial Framing**\n```\nYou are conducting a deep codebase research audit. DO NOT modify any files \nexcept in .opencode/research/. Follow this protocol:\n\n1. FRAME: Use repomix pack_codebase (compressed) on the target directory.\n   Read the directory structure and metrics from the response.\n   Use grep_repomix_output to locate entry points (index.ts, main exports).\n\n2. MAP: Launch parallel explore subagents to map each major module.",
        "characterCount": 493,
        "estimatedTokens": 124,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#43-concrete-orchestrator-prompt-templates-47"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 48,
        "heading": "4.3 Concrete Orchestrator Prompt Templates",
        "startLine": 398,
        "endLine": 410,
        "content": "Each agent should return: file list, key exports, class hierarchy, \n   dependency imports.\n\n3. TRACE: Use LSP (if available) or grep to trace cross-module dependencies.\n   Use codesearch/websearch for external library documentation.\n\n4. SYNTHESIZE: Write findings to .opencode/research/<topic>.md using apply_patch.\n   Generate a skill using repomix generate_skill for persistent reference.\n\nCurrent target: [REPO_PATH]\nFocus area: [SPECIFIC_SUBSYSTEM]\n```",
        "characterCount": 456,
        "estimatedTokens": 114,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#43-concrete-orchestrator-prompt-templates-48"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 49,
        "heading": "4.3 Concrete Orchestrator Prompt Templates",
        "startLine": 411,
        "endLine": 423,
        "content": "**Template: Subagent Research Delegation**\n```\nTask(\n  description=\"Map [MODULE_NAME] architecture\",\n  subagent_type=\"explore\",\n  prompt=`RESEARCH ONLY - no file modifications.\n\n  Target: [REPO_PATH]/src/[MODULE]/\n  \n  Execute these steps:\n  1. glob({ pattern: \"**/*.ts\", path: \"[REPO_PATH]/src/[MODULE]\" })\n  2. For each key file, grep for exports: grep({ pattern: \"^export\", path: \"[file]\" })\n  3. Read the main entry point with read({ filePath: \"[entry]\", limit: 200 })",
        "characterCount": 472,
        "estimatedTokens": 118,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#43-concrete-orchestrator-prompt-templates-49"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 50,
        "heading": "4.3 Concrete Orchestrator Prompt Templates",
        "startLine": 424,
        "endLine": 442,
        "content": "4. If files are large, use offset reading to get specific sections\n  \n  Return a structured report:\n  - File tree with descriptions\n  - Key exports and their types\n  - Import dependencies (what this module depends on)\n  - Exported APIs (what other modules consume)\n  - Patterns observed (singleton, factory, observer, etc.)\n  `\n)\n```\n\n**Template: Cross-Repo Dependency Tracing**\n```\nTask(\n  description=\"Trace MCP integration across repos\",\n  subagent_type=\"explore\",\n  prompt=`RESEARCH ONLY.",
        "characterCount": 492,
        "estimatedTokens": 123,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#43-concrete-orchestrator-prompt-templates-50"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 51,
        "heading": "4.3 Concrete Orchestrator Prompt Templates",
        "startLine": 443,
        "endLine": 454,
        "content": "I have already packed these repos:\n  - hivemind-plugin outputId: [ID1]\n  - oh-my-openagent outputId: [ID2]\n  \n  Use grep_repomix_output on both outputs to find:\n  1. All MCP server registrations: pattern \"registerTool|McpServer|mcp\"\n  2. All tool definitions: pattern \"Tool\\\\.define|registerTool\"\n  3. All plugin hooks: pattern \"hook|lifecycle|register|dispose\"\n  \n  Cross-reference the patterns. Report:\n  - Shared abstractions between repos\n  - Divergent implementations",
        "characterCount": 472,
        "estimatedTokens": 118,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#43-concrete-orchestrator-prompt-templates-51"
      },

        "content": "- Integration points where hivemind-plugin could adopt openagent patterns\n  `\n)\n```",
        "characterCount": 83,

        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#43-concrete-orchestrator-prompt-templates-52"

        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 53,
        "heading": "4.4 Shell Output Chaining & $Argument Patterns",
        "startLine": 460,
        "endLine": 464,
        "content": "### 4.4 Shell Output Chaining & $Argument Patterns\n\nUse `bash` tool for git-based research and piped analysis:\n\n```bash",
        "characterCount": 119,
        "estimatedTokens": 30,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#44-shell-output-chaining-argument-patterns-53"

        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 54,
        "heading": "Git archaeology -- find most-changed files (hot spots for refactoring)",
        "startLine": 465,
        "endLine": 467,
        "content": "# Git archaeology -- find most-changed files (hot spots for refactoring)\ngit log --since=\"6 months ago\" --name-only --pretty=format: | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -30",
        "characterCount": 171,
        "estimatedTokens": 43,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#git-archaeology-find-most-changed-files-hot-spots-for-refactoring-54"
      },

        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 55,
        "heading": "Find all cross-repo imports",
        "startLine": 468,
        "endLine": 470,
        "content": "# Find all cross-repo imports\ngrep -rn \"from.*@opencode\\|from.*@hivemind\\|from.*openagent\" src/ --include=\"*.ts\"",
        "characterCount": 112,
        "estimatedTokens": 28,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#find-all-cross-repo-imports-55"
      },

      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 60,
        "heading": "4.5 Auto-Parsed Prompting & Command Chaining",
        "startLine": 485,
        "endLine": 501,
        "content": "### 4.5 Auto-Parsed Prompting & Command Chaining\n\nOpencode supports slash commands via Task tool: [1-cite-26](#1-cite-26) \n\n```\nTask(description=\"Run custom check\", prompt=\"/check-architecture src/delegation/\", subagent_type=\"general\")\n```\n\nCustom modes can restrict tool access for research-only sessions: [1-cite-27](#1-cite-27) \n\n```json\n{\n  \"mode\": {\n    \"research\": {\n      \"prompt\": \"{file:./prompts/research-protocol.txt}\",\n      \"tools\": {\n        \"write\": false, \"edit\": false, \"bash\": true,",
        "characterCount": 500,
        "estimatedTokens": 125,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#45-auto-parsed-prompting-command-chaining-60"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 61,
        "heading": "4.5 Auto-Parsed Prompting & Command Chaining",
        "startLine": 502,
        "endLine": 511,
        "content": "\"read\": true, \"grep\": true, \"glob\": true,\n        \"list\": true, \"webfetch\": true, \"websearch\": true\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 128,
        "estimatedTokens": 32,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#45-auto-parsed-prompting-command-chaining-61"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 62,
        "heading": "Part V: Integrated Workflow -- Long-Haul Audit Protocol",
        "startLine": 512,
        "endLine": 513,
        "content": "## Part V: Integrated Workflow -- Long-Haul Audit Protocol",
        "characterCount": 58,
        "estimatedTokens": 15,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#part-v-integrated-workflow-long-haul-audit-protocol-62"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 63,
        "heading": "5.1 The Disk-Based Synthesis Chain",
        "startLine": 514,
        "endLine": 531,
        "content": "### 5.1 The Disk-Based Synthesis Chain\n\nAligned with your protocol's `Batch 1 -> Disk Write -> Batch 2 -> ...` pattern:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph \"Batch 1: Survey\"\n        S1[\"pack_codebase (compressed)\"]\n        S2[\"grep for architecture markers\"]\n        S3[\"write -> .opencode/research/01-survey.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Batch 2: Deep Dive\"\n        D1[\"read 01-survey.md\"]\n        D2[\"3x explore subagents (parallel)\"]\n        D3[\"write -> .opencode/research/02-modules.md\"]\n    end",
        "characterCount": 500,
        "estimatedTokens": 125,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#51-the-disk-based-synthesis-chain-63"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 64,
        "heading": "5.1 The Disk-Based Synthesis Chain",
        "startLine": 532,
        "endLine": 549,
        "content": "subgraph \"Batch 3: Cross-Reference\"\n        X1[\"read 02-modules.md\"]\n        X2[\"pack_remote_repository (dependencies)\"]\n        X3[\"codesearch + websearch\"]\n        X4[\"write -> .opencode/research/03-cross-deps.md\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Batch 4: Persist\"\n        P1[\"read 03-cross-deps.md\"]\n        P2[\"generate_skill (repomix)\"]\n        P3[\"write -> .opencode/research/04-synthesis.md\"]\n    end\n\n    S3 --> D1\n    D3 --> X1\n    X4 --> P1\n```",
        "characterCount": 445,
        "estimatedTokens": 112,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#51-the-disk-based-synthesis-chain-64"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md",
        "index": 65,
        "heading": "5.2 TodoWrite for Multi-Cycle Tracking",
        "startLine": 550,
        "endLine": 558,
        "content": "### 5.2 TodoWrite for Multi-Cycle Tracking\n\nUse `todowrite` to maintain research state across cycles: [1-cite-28](#1-cite-28) \n\n```jsonc\ntodowrite({ todos: [\n  { \"id\": \"1\", \"content\": \"Survey hivemind-plugin architecture\", \"status\": \"completed\" },\n  { \"id\": \"2\", \"content\": \"Map delegation layer in detail\", \"status\": \"in_progress\" },\n  { \"id\": \"3\", \"content\": \"Trace MCP integration points\", \"status\": \"pending\" },",
        "characterCount": 415,
        "estimatedTokens": 104,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#52-todowrite-for-multi-cycle-tracking-65"
      },

        "index": 66,
        "heading": "5.2 TodoWrite for Multi-Cycle Tracking",

        "content": "{ \"id\": \"4\", \"content\": \"Cross-reference with openagent patterns\", \"status\": \"pending\" },\n  { \"id\": \"5\", \"content\": \"Generate synthesis skill\", \"status\": \"pending\" }\n]})\n```",
        "characterCount": 173,
        "estimatedTokens": 44,
        "id": ".opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md#52-todowrite-for-multi-cycle-tracking-66"

## Goal
- Act as GSD-UAT Team B end-user tester validating all Hivemind harness custom tools/features under OpenCode runtime, organized into 3 feature paths × 3 phases across 13 batches, writing all results to disk with `team-b` marker.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Never read project source code — rely solely on system responses and traceable results
- Never test MCP tools — only Hivemind harness tools
- No assumptions, suggestions, or diagnoses — act strictly as blind end-user
- Must complete end-to-end lifecycle before concluding pass/fail
- All outputs, folders, files tagged with `team-b`
- Write to disk frequently — context will be lost on compact
- L0 orchestrator role with 3-level delegation depth (L0→L1→L2)
- 3 feature paths: (1) agent-callable tools, (2) runtime programmatic, (3) governance/config
- 3 phases: individual live testing → cross-primitive integration → workflow audit

## Progress
### Done
- **Phase 1 complete** — all 3 paths individually tested across batches 1-6
- **Batch 1-3 (Path 1: Agent Tools)**: nl-route, delegate-task, delegation-status, todowrite, stacked workflow, L0→L1→L2 chain, prompt-skim, prompt-analyze, session-journal-export, session-patch, hivemind-doc — all PASS except L1→L2 chain BLOCKED
- **Batch 4 (CRUD Deep)**: hivemind-agent-work-create (L3_STATIC_REVIEW) PASS, agent-work-export (JSON+markdown) PASS, trajectory full CRUD (attach/checkpoint/event/traverse/close) all PASS, pressure (detect/inspect_tool_catalog/attach_event) all PASS, command-engine (discover/analyze_contract/render_context/route_preview/transform_messages) all PASS, configure-primitive list (18 commands) PASS, validate-restart FAIL (14/18 commands reference non-existent agents)
- **Batch 5 (Path 2: Runtime Programmatic)**: run-background-command full lifecycle PASS, prompt-skim→analyze pipeline PASS, session-patch validation PASS
- **Batch 6 (Path 3: Governance)**: configure-primitive read/inspect PASS, list skills (57) PASS, sdk-supervisor heartbeat+diagnostics PASS, nl-route edge cases PASS, cross-governance chain PASS
- **Batch 7 (Phase 2: Cross-Primitive — delegation+PTY+journal+governance)**: SDK delegation `08aff8a9` to hm-l2-general completed 27.0s PASS, PTY `6b2a3686` ran concurrently on separate queueKey PASS, trajectory checkpoint + pressure event + journal export all linked PASS — proves two different execution modes (sdk/agent-delegation vs pty/command-process) run on independent concurrency lanes
- **Batch 8 (Phase 2: Cross-Primitive — command-engine+configure-primitive+doc)**: command-engine discover/analyze_contract/route_preview/transform_messages all PASS for `deep-research-synthesis-repomix`, configure-primitive list (scope=project) PASS, hivemind-doc chunk on command .md file PASS, hivemind-doc search returned 0 matches (md files not plain-text searchable) — all cross-primitive operations at tier 0 steady PASS

### In Progress
- **Batch 7-8 results not yet written to disk** — need to persist before compact

### Blocked
- **L1→L2 delegation chain**: `hm-l1-coordinator` lacks `delegate-task` in its tool permissions — architecturally intended but non-functional due to configuration gap.

## Key Decisions
- Testing proceeds incrementally: individual tools first, then stacked, then cross-primitive
- Delegations used for validation are simple echo tasks to isolate tool behavior from agent complexity
- Critical findings written to separate files for audit trail
- L1 chain block documented as configuration gap, not code bug
- validate-restart failure categorized as config drift, not tool malfunction
- configure-primitive `scope` must be `project` or `global` (not `local`) — learned from Batch 8 error

## Next Steps
1. Write Batch 7-8 results to disk immediately (risk of compact)
2. Phase 3 batches 9-11: Workflow audit, compliance, production-readiness
3. Phase 3 final: Output ultimate by-phase audit with debts, gaps, and new workstream proposals

## Critical Context
- **Phase 1 Score**: 78 PASS, 3 FAIL (1 expected: validate-restart config drift, 2 expected: PTY human ID access denied)
- **Phase 2 Batch 7**: SDK delegation `08aff8a9` completed (27.0s, resumable), PTY `6b2a3686` (pty-c84e0e4c) concurrent, queueKeys differ (agent:hm-l2-general vs category:command)
- **Phase 2 Batch 8**: command-engine route_preview returns executable=false (preview only, not execution), transform_messages appends system message with exclusions (broad-system-transform, process-launch, command-execution)
- **CRITICAL-4.1**: validate-restart found 14/18 commands (78%) reference non-existent agents
- **FINDING-5.1**: run-background-command `output`/`terminate` reject human sessionId — must use internal PTY ID
- **FINDING-4.2**: pressure attach_event successfully wrote to a CLOSED trajectory — no guardrail preventing mutation of closed trajectories
- **FINDING-6.1**: nl-route matches first keyword only, confidence=0.333 for multi-keyword queries
- **FINDING-6.2**: hm-l2-planning-persistence/SKILL.md has invalid frontmatter
- **FINDING-8.1**: hivemind-doc search on `.opencode/commands/` directory returned 0 matches for "command definition deep-research" — doc search doesn't index command .md files effectively
- **86 total agents**: 32 hm-*, 10 hf-*, 34 gsd-*, 10 internal; 57 skills; 18 commands
- **Parent session**: `ses_20bf160c2ffeK7CMIypDMSGK0h`
- **Trajectories**: `traj_uat_team_b_batch4` (closed), `traj_uat_team_b_batch6` (active, has batch 7+8 events/checkpoints)
- **Pressure**: all tools tier=0 band=steady at this session load
- **configure-primitive**: scope must be "project"|"global" (not "local"); stateSurface=opencode-primitive, authority=write
- **run-background-command valid actions**: run, output, input, list, terminate (NOT start/read)
- **hivemind-command-engine route_preview**: requires `commandName` param (not `arguments`); returns pressure tier+band+outcome alongside route+contract+context+transform
- **hivemind-agent-work-create evidence levels**: L1_RUNTIME_PROOF, L2_AUTOMATED_TEST, L3_STATIC_REVIEW, L4_IMPLEMENTATION_TRACE, L5_DOCUMENTATION

1: # GSD-UAT Team B — Master Test Plan
2: **Date:** 2026-05-05
3: **Team:** Team B (parallel comparison track)
4: **Scope:** All Hivemind harness custom tools & features (NOT MCP tools)
5: 
6: ---
7: 
8: ## Agent Landscape (86 total)
9: - **hm-***: 32 agents (L0: 1, L1: 1, L2: 30)
10: - **hf-***: 10 agents (L0: 1, L1: 1, L2: 8)
11: - **gsd-***: 34 agents (specialist subagents)
12: - **internal**: 10 agents (build, conductor, critic, general, etc.)
13: 
14: ---
15: 
16: ## 3 Feature Paths
17: 
18: ### Path 1: Agent-Callable Tools & Skills (Deterministic invocation)
19: **Sub-groups:**
20: 1. **Task Management**: delegate-task, delegation-status, nl-route, todowrite
21: 2. **Delegation/Coordination**: L0→L1→L2 delegation chain, WaiterModel dispatch, dual-signal completion
22: 3. **Context & Memory**: session-journal-export, prompt-skim, prompt-analyze, session-patch
23: 4. **Hivemind CRUD**: configure-primitive, hivemind-doc, hivemind-trajectory, hivemind-pressure, hivemind-sdk-supervisor, hivemind-command-engine, hivemind-agent-work-create/export
24: 
25: ### Path 2: Runtime Programmatic (Auto-wiring, hooks, transformations)
26: **Sub-groups:**
27: 1. **Hook Composition**: session.created, session.updated, session.deleted, session.idle, tool.execute.after
28: 2. **Prompt Transformation**: prompt-skim/analyze pipeline, prompt-repackager
29: 3. **Auto-routing**: nl-route natural language routing, lineage-router skill dispatch
30: 4. **Lifecycle State Machine**: SessionLifecyclePhase transitions, completion detection
31: 
32: ### Path 3: Governance, Permissions, Registry & Configuration
33: **Sub-groups:**
34: 1. **Permissions**: tool capability matrix enforcement, depth-based access control
35: 2. **Registry**: configure-primitive (agent/command/skill CRUD), validate-restart
36: 3. **Stack & Chain**: skill loading lineage, tech-context-compliance, tech-stack-ingest
37: 4. **Configuration**: runtime-policy, circuit breaker thresholds, budget policies
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 3 Phases
42: 
43: ### Phase 1: Individual & Single-Group Live Testing
44: - Each tool tested individually with real invocations
45: - Raw system response logging
46: - Basic → Advanced use cases per tool
47: - Stack within sub-groups for combined testing
48: 
49: ### Phase 2: Cross-Primitive Integration Testing
50: - Tools tested in combination with correct OpenCode primitives
51: - Agents + Skills + Commands working together
52: - Cross-path interactions (Path 1 + Path 2, Path 2 + Path 3, etc.)
53: - Multi-level delegation chains (L0→L1→L2)
54: 
55: ### Phase 3: Workflow & Audit
56: - Full workflow runs (end-to-end lifecycle)
57: - Integration compliance checks
58: - OpenCode SDK compliance validation
59: - Production-readiness assessment
60: - Gap/debt analysis → new workstream proposals
61: 
62: ---
63: 
64: ## Test Execution Rules
65: 1. **Never read project source code** — rely on system responses only
66: 2. **Never test MCP tools** — only Hivemind harness tools
67: 3. **Log every system response** — raw output to disk
68: 4. **No assumptions** — act as blind end-user
69: 5. **End-to-end lifecycle** — no partial conclusions
70: 6. **Team-B marker** — all outputs tagged with `team-b`
71: 7. **Write to disk frequently** — context will be lost on compact
72: 
73: ---
74: 
75: ## Batch Processing
76: - Phase 1: 6 batches (2 per path × 3 paths)
77: - Phase 2: 4 batches (cross-path combinations)
78: - Phase 3: 3 batches (audit, compliance, production)
79: - **Total: 13 batches**

(End of file - total 79 lines)
</content>

8: ## Test 8.1: Command-Engine Discovery Chain
9: 
10: | Step | Tool | Input | Result | Verdict |
11: |------|------|-------|--------|---------|
12: | 1 | hivemind-command-engine | action=discover, arguments=deep-research-synthesis-repomix | Returned 11 commands matching argument (deep-init, deep-research-synthesis-repomix, harness-audit, harness-doctor, hf-absorb, hf-audit, hf-configure, hf-create, hf-prompt-enhance, hf-prompt-enhance-to-plan, hf-stack) | PASS |
13: | 2 | hivemind-command-engine | action=analyze_contract, commandName=deep-research-synthesis-repomix | valid=true, acceptsArguments=false, failureStates=[missing_command, invalid_contract, pressure_blocked, context_overflow], contextNeeds=[bounded-context, pressure-decision] | PASS |
14: | 3 | hivemind-command-engine | action=route_preview, commandName=deep-research-synthesis-repomix | executable=false, pressure=tier0/steady/allow, route.status=ready, exclusions=[broad-system-transform, process-launch, command-execution] | PASS |
15: | 4 | hivemind-command-engine | action=transform_messages, commandName=deep-research-synthesis-repomix | Messages transformed with exclusions=[broad-system-transform, process-launch, command-execution] | PASS |
16: 
17: ## Test 8.2: Cross-Tool Integration (Doc + Configure)
18: 
19: | Step | Tool | Input | Result | Verdict |
20: |------|------|-------|--------|---------|
21: | 1 | hivemind-doc | action=search, path=.opencode/commands, query="command definition deep-research" | matches=[] (0 results) | PASS (empty expected) |
22: | 2 | hivemind-doc | action=chunk, path=.opencode/commands/deep-research-synthesis-repomix.md, maxCharacters=500 | 67 chunks returned with heading, startLine, endLine, characterCount, estimatedTokens | PASS |
23: | 3 | configure-primitive | action=list, scope=project (first attempt: scope=local → ERROR) | 18 commands listed, warning about invalid skill frontmatter | PASS |
24: | 4 | hivemind-pressure | action=attach_event, tier=0 | Pressure evidence attached to trajectory | PASS |
25: | 5 | hivemind-trajectory | action=checkpoint | Checkpoint created with batch 8 summary | PASS |
26: 
27: **Key Observation:** `hivemind-doc search` returned 0 matches for command files — doc search appears to only index plain text content, not Markdown files in `.opencode/commands/`. However, `hivemind-doc chunk` successfully parsed the `.md` file into 67 structured chunks.
28: 
29: ## Parameter Validation Findings
30: 
31: | Tool | Parameter | Invalid Value | Valid Values | Verdict |
32: |------|-----------|---------------|--------------|---------|
33: | configure-primitive | scope | "local" | "project"\|"global" | ERROR (correct validation) |
34: | hivemind-command-engine | route_preview | arguments=<value> | commandName=<value> | ERROR (correct validation) |
35:

37:

39: |--------|-------|

42: | FAIL | 0 |

44: 
45: ## Findings
46: 
47: ### FINDING-8.1: Doc Search Returns Empty for .md Command Files
48: - **Severity:** Low
49: - **Detail:** `hivemind-doc search` on `.opencode/commands/` directory returns 0 matches for any query. `hivemind-doc chunk` on individual `.md` files works correctly (67 chunks).
50: - **Impact:** Doc search cannot discover command content; must use `chunk` with specific file paths.
51: 
52: ### FINDING-8.2: configure-primitive Scope Validation
53: - **Severity:** Info
54: - **Detail:** `scope=local` is rejected with clear error message. Valid values are `project` and `global` only. This is correct validation behavior, not a bug.

<skill_content name="hm-l2-gate-orchestrator">
# Skill: hm-l2-gate-orchestrator

## The Iron Law

```
Gates execute in fixed order: lifecycle → spec → evidence. No gate is skipped. No gate runs out of order.
```

# Gate Orchestrator

## Overview

Orchestrate the quality gate triad as a fixed-order pipeline. Each gate is an independent skill that produces a PASS/FAIL verdict. This skill sequences them, propagates results, and renders a unified verdict. It does NOT perform gate logic — it routes to the gate skills and enforces ordering.

**Three gates in strict sequence:**

| Gate | Skill | Checks | Duration |
|------|-------|--------|----------|
| Gate 1 | `gate-lifecycle-integration` | 9-surface mutation authority, CQRS boundaries, actor hierarchy, event-driven wiring | ~2 min |
| Gate 2 | `gate-spec-compliance` | Bidirectional traceability, gap detection (4 types), EARS acceptance criteria | ~3 min |
| Gate 3 | `gate-evidence-truth` | L1-L5 evidence hierarchy, mock detection, runtime proof verification | ~2 min |

**HALT rule:** If any gate returns FAIL, stop the pipeline. Produce a remediation report. Do NOT proceed to the next gate.

## On Load

1. Read `references/gate-flow.md` — the complete gate flow diagram with decision points
2. Identify the target: which files, modules, or phases to validate
3. Determine the gate context: code-review, phase-audit, milestone-verification, or deployment-readiness

## Trigger Phrases

- "run the gates" / "run quality gates"
- "gate orchestration" / "orchestrate the triad"
- "quality triad" / "full gate pass"
- "route gates" / "gate sequence"
- "phase audit gate" / "verify against gates"
- "gate check before deploy" / "triad verification"
- "quality gate pipeline" / "gate pipeline"

## Gate Flow

Follow this pipeline in strict order. Each gate receives the target artifacts and context from the previous gate.

```
- [ ] STEP 1: PREPARE — Identify target artifacts, load gate context
- [ ] STEP 2: GATE 1 — Route to gate-lifecycle-integration
  → If FAIL: STOP → remediation report → exit
  → If PASS: continue
- [ ] STEP 3: GATE 2 — Route to gate-spec-compliance (receives Gate 1 verdict)
  → If FAIL: STOP → remediation report → exit
  → If PASS: continue
- [ ] STEP 4: GATE 3 — Route to gate-evidence-truth (receives Gate 1 + 2 verdicts)
  → If FAIL: STOP → remediation report → exit
  → If PASS: unified verdict
- [ ] STEP 5: VERDICT — Render unified PASS/FAIL with evidence summary
- [ ] STEP 6: REMEDIATION — If any gate failed, produce structured fix plan
```

### STEP 1: Prepare

Identify what to validate:

- **Target artifacts:** List specific files, modules, or phase directories
- **Gate context:** One of: code-review, phase-audit, milestone-verification, deployment-readiness
- **Prior gate results:** If resuming from a failed gate, load previous results

### STEP 2: Gate 1 — Lifecycle Integration

Route to `gate-lifecycle-integration`. Pass:

- Target artifacts (file paths)
- Gate context (code-review / phase-audit / milestone / deployment)
- Any prior phase context (PLAN.md, SPEC.md)

**On PASS:** Record verdict. Continue to Gate 2.
**On FAIL:** Record failure mode. Skip Gates 2-3. Produce remediation report.

### STEP 3: Gate 2 — Spec Compliance

Route to `gate-spec-compliance`. Pass:

- Target artifacts
- Gate 1 verdict (lifecycle compliance evidence)
- SPEC.md or requirements document for traceability

**On PASS:** Record verdict. Continue to Gate 3.
**On FAIL:** Record failure mode (gap type, missing criteria). Skip Gate 3. Produce remediation report.

### STEP 4: Gate 3 — Evidence Truth

Route to `gate-evidence-truth`. Pass:

- Target artifacts
- Gate 1 + 2 verdicts (lifecycle + spec evidence)
- Implementation evidence (test results, runtime output, deployment artifacts)

**On PASS:** Record verdict. All three gates clear.
**On FAIL:** Record failure mode (insufficient evidence, mock detection). Produce remediation report.

### STEP 5: Verdict

Render a unified verdict:

| Outcome | Condition | Report |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| **PASS** | All 3 gates PASS | Summary: lifecycle ✅ spec ✅ evidence ✅ |
| **FAIL** | Any gate FAILS | Detailed remediation with gate-specific fixes |
| **PARTIAL** | Gate halted mid-execution | Context: which gate, why halted, what to resume |

### STEP 6: Remediation

If any gate failed, produce a structured remediation report:

```markdown
## Gate Remediation Report

**Failed Gate:** [Gate 1/2/3]
**Failure Mode:** [lifecycle-breach / spec-gap / evidence-insufficient]
**Affected Files:** [list]
**Remediation Steps:**
1. [specific fix action]
2. [specific fix action]
**Re-run Command:** Route to hm-gate-orchestrator after fixes applied
```

## Usage Protocol

### For Agents

When an agent needs to validate work through the quality triad:

1. Load this skill (`hm-gate-orchestrator`)
2. Call this skill with the target artifacts and gate context
3. Follow the 6-step pipeline exactly
4. Return the verdict to the calling agent

### For Integration Points

Three skills consume this orchestrator:

| Consumer | When | Expected Outcome |
|----------|------|-----------------|
| `hm-production-readiness` | After deployment safety verification | Unified gate verdict for deployment approval |
| `hm-requirements-analysis` | When gate validation reveals requirement insufficiency | Requirement gap remediation |
| `hm-roadmap-maintainability` | During milestone planning gates | Milestone approval with gate evidence |

## Self-Correction

### Anti-Pattern 1: Skipped Gate

**Detection:** Pipeline completes in fewer than 3 gate invocations.
**Correction:** Re-run from the skipped gate. Document why the skip occurred and whether it was intentional (it should never be).

### Anti-Pattern 2: Wrong Order

**Detection:** Gate 2 or Gate 3 runs before Gate 1, or Gate 3 runs before Gate 2.
**Correction:** Discard results. Restart from Gate 1. The ordering is mandatory because each gate's evidence feeds the next.

### Anti-Pattern 3: Mock as Evidence

**Detection:** Gate 3 (evidence-truth) reports L1/L2 evidence but investigation reveals mocked data flows.
**Correction:** Do not lower the evidence level. Mark as FAIL with remediation: "Replace mock with live integration test or runtime proof."

### Anti-Pattern 4: Gate Shopping

**Detection:** Pipeline stops at a failed gate, then the caller tries to re-run only the passing gates while skipping the failed one.
**Correction:** All three gates MUST pass. A failed gate means the work is not ready. Fix the failure, then re-run the full pipeline from Gate 1.

## Quality Contract (HMQUAL Compliance)

| HMQUAL | Compliance | Evidence |
|--------|-----------|----------|
| HMQUAL-01 | Trigger phrases ≥7 in description | 12 trigger phrases in description |
| HMQUAL-02 | Self-correction with 4 anti-patterns | Skipped Gate, Wrong Order, Mock as Evidence, Gate Shopping |
| HMQUAL-03 | Cross-references to sibling skills | 6 cross-references (3 gate skills + 3 consumer skills) |
| HMQUAL-04 | Progressive disclosure | SKILL.md (this file) + references/gate-flow.md |
| HMQUAL-05 | Evals with 3 scenarios | evals/evals.json — 3 trigger scenarios |
| HMQUAL-06 | Metrics scorecard | metrics/rich-gate-scorecard.md |
| HMQUAL-07 | Iron law enforcement | "Gates execute in fixed order. No gate is skipped." |
| HMQUAL-08 | Honest RICH scoring | Scored in metrics/rich-gate-scorecard.md |

## Cross-References

| Related Skill | Boundary |
|---------------|----------|
| `gate-lifecycle-integration` | Gate 1 — 9-surface mutation authority. This skill routes to it. |
| `gate-spec-compliance` | Gate 2 — Bidirectional traceability. This skill routes to it. |
| `gate-evidence-truth` | Gate 3 — L1-L5 evidence hierarchy. This skill routes to it. |
| `hm-production-readiness` | Consumer — calls this orchestrator for deployment gates |
| `hm-requirements-analysis` | Consumer — calls this orchestrator for requirement validation gates |
| `hm-roadmap-maintainability` | Consumer — calls this orchestrator for milestone planning gates |
| `hm-lineage-router` | Routes quality tasks to this skill via the quality bundle |

<skill_content name="gate-l3-evidence-truth">
# Skill: gate-l3-evidence-truth

## The Iron Law

```
No gate passes on documentation alone. Evidence must be classified, honest, and runtime-verified.
```

# Gate Evidence Truth

Terminal gate in the quality-gate triad. Receives from `gate-spec-compliance` after spec alignment clears. If evidence truth PASSES, all three gates (spec, lifecycle, evidence) are satisfied and work may proceed to merge/release.

## On Load

1. Read `references/evaluation-checklist.md` — per-dimension audit criteria
2. Read `references/anti-patterns.md` — known evidence anti-patterns and detection
3. Read `references/harness-verification-trees.md` — module-specific verification protocols

## Do NOT Load

Skip this skill when:

- Performing spec alignment checks — use `gate-spec-compliance` instead
- Checking lifecycle coverage — use `gate-lifecycle-integration` instead
- Running research or exploration workflows (not gate verification)
- The calling workflow has not passed `gate-spec-compliance` yet
- No implementation artifacts exist to evaluate

## Evidence Hierarchy

Classify every evidence artifact into exactly one level:

| Level | Source | Example |
|-------|--------|---------|
| **L1** | Live OpenCode runtime session | Feature exercised in actual OpenCode instance |
| **L2** | Continuity record from live run | Delegation/session record captured from real execution |
| **L3** | Integration test passing | Tests hitting real SDK boundaries (not mocked) |
| **L4** | Unit test passing | Tests that mock SDK boundaries |
| **L5** | Documentation/historical summary | Specs, design docs, conversation summaries |

**L5 alone never passes any gate.**

## Gate Type → Minimum Evidence Level

| Gate Type | Minimum Level | Required Evidence |
|-----------|---------------|-------------------|
| PR review | L3 | At least integration tests hitting real boundaries |
| Phase completion | L2 | Continuity record from real run |
| Merge | L2 | Continuity record from real run |
| Milestone completion | L2 + one L1 | Continuity record plus at least one live session proof |
| Release/deployment | L1 | Live runtime proof required |

## Contextual Perspective Activation

Determine the current context, then activate the appropriate evaluation lens:

| Context | Primary Lens | Secondary Lens | Emphasis |
|---------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| Code review | Dev | Architect | Test integrity, mock coverage |
| Phase audit | Architect | PM | Evidence hierarchy compliance, regression |
| Milestone verification | PM | Architect | Runtime proof, completion honesty |
| Integration check | Architect | Dev | Live verification, cross-boundary testing |
| Deployment readiness | All three | — | Full evidence verification across all dimensions |

Load `references/perspective-rubrics.md` for per-lens evaluation criteria.

## Core Evaluation Workflow

Follow this checklist in order:

```
- [ ] STEP 1: GATHER — Collect all evidence artifacts for the claim
- [ ] STEP 2: CLASSIFY — Assign L1–L5 to each artifact using evidence hierarchy
- [ ] STEP 3: CHECK MINIMUM — Does highest evidence level meet gate-type minimum?
- [ ] STEP 4: DETECT MOCKS — Any mock-only claims for integration surfaces?
- [ ] STEP 5: CHECK COMPLETION HONESTY — Any completion claims without backing evidence?
- [ ] STEP 6: REGRESSION CHECK — Any cross-phase regression risk from dependency graph?
- [ ] STEP 7: ANTI-PATTERN SCAN — Match evidence against anti-pattern catalog
- [ ] STEP 8: VERDICT — Render PASS or FAIL with remediation plan
```

See `references/evaluation-workflow-detail.md` for detailed step procedures, mock detection commands, and classification guidance.

## Cross-Skill Routing

### Triad Flow (lifecycle → spec → evidence)

```
gate-lifecycle-integration (ENTRY — checks lifecycle correctness)
    ↓ PASS
gate-spec-compliance (MIDDLE — checks spec alignment)
    ↓ PASS
gate-evidence-truth ← YOU ARE HERE (TERMINAL)
    ↓
    ├─ PASS → ALL 3 GATES CLEAR → proceed to merge/release
    └─ FAIL → STOP → produce remediation plan → route to appropriate fix skill
```

Triad backward references, evidence collection handoff from `hm-production-readiness`, and full remediation routing are in `references/cross-skill-routing-detail.md`.

### Related Skills

| Related Skill | Role | Interaction |
|---------------|------|-------------|
| `gate-lifecycle-integration` | Entry gate | Must pass before spec compliance runs |
| `gate-spec-compliance` | Middle gate | Routes to this gate on PASS |
| `hm-production-readiness` | Evidence collector | Feeds structured L1-L5 evidence into this gate |
| `hm-cross-cutting-change` | Mock honesty | Remediation target for mock-only failures |
| `hm-debug` | Root-cause investigation | Remediation for insufficient evidence level |
| `hm-completion-looping` | Loop-back control | Uses this gate's verdict for loop-back decisions |
| `hm-coordinating-loop` | Orchestration re-dispatch | Re-dispatches when evidence or regression gaps found |
| `hm-gate-orchestrator` | Triad lifecycle manager | Orchestrates triad sequencing, state persistence, cross-gate handoff |

## Adopted Patterns

Synthesizes three proven methodologies: **Anthropic Gather→Act→Verify**, **Google Testing Pyramid (70-20-10)**, **GRADE Evidence Framework**. See `references/adopted-patterns.md` for full synthesis.

## Self-Correction

### Mode 1: When No Evidence Exists

Do not downgrade the gate minimum. Report FAIL with explicit instructions: "Produce [L2/L1] evidence by [running integration test / capturing live session / verifying in OpenCode instance]."

### Mode 2: When Evidence Is Ambiguous

Classify at the lower level. A test that mocks some but not all SDK boundaries is L4, not L3. A continuity record that may or may not be from a live session is L5, not L2.

### Mode 3: When the User Overrides

Document the waiver in the evidence report with: which gate failed, what evidence was missing, and the user's rationale. The override must be explicit — never silently waive.

### Mode 4: When Evidence Contradicts Itself

If two artifacts provide conflicting evidence for the same claim (e.g., a passing test but a failing continuity record), treat the contradiction as a regression signal. Classify both artifacts at their individual levels, mark the claim as UNVERIFIED, and require reconciliation before rendering PASS. Never average or weight conflicting evidence — resolve the contradiction or FAIL.

## Gate Orchestrator Integration

This gate participates in the triad orchestrated by `hm-gate-orchestrator`. The orchestrator manages triad sequencing, state persistence, and cross-gate handoff. When invoked within an orchestrator workflow, this skill receives a pre-built evidence bundle and returns a structured verdict. See `hm-gate-orchestrator` for full triad lifecycle management.

## Reference Files

| File | Content |
|------|---------|
| `references/evaluation-checklist.md` | Per-dimension audit criteria |
| `references/evaluation-workflow-detail.md` | Detailed STEPS 1-8 procedures and detection commands |
| `references/cross-skill-routing-detail.md` | Triad backward refs, evidence handoff, remediation routing |
| `references/perspective-rubrics.md` | PM/Architect/Dev rubrics with contextual activation |
| `references/anti-patterns.md` | Full anti-pattern catalog with severity and detection |
| `references/adopted-patterns.md` | Synthesized patterns from Anthropic, Google, GRADE |
| `references/harness-verification-trees.md` | Module-specific verification trees |
| `templates/evidence-report.md` | Standardized report template |
| `scripts/run-evidence-check.sh` | Deterministic evidence classification checker |
| `references/project-adapter-guide.md` | Project-to-harness path mapping adapter |
| `metrics/rich-gate-scorecard.md` | RICH-8 quality scorecard |
| `evals/evals.json` | Test scenarios for this skill |

        "startLine": 30,

        "characterCount": 122,
        "estimatedTokens": 31,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#the-iron-law-1"
