```
        "index": 2,
        "heading": "Gate Evidence Truth",
        "startLine": 36,
        "endLine": 39,
        "content": "# Gate Evidence Truth\n\nTerminal gate in the quality-gate triad. Receives from `gate-spec-compliance` after spec alignment clears. If evidence truth PASSES, all three gates (spec, lifecycle, evidence) are satisfied and work may proceed to merge/release.",
        "characterCount": 252,
        "estimatedTokens": 63,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#gate-evidence-truth-2"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 3,
        "heading": "On Load",
        "startLine": 40,
        "endLine": 45,
        "content": "## On Load\n\n1. Read `references/evaluation-checklist.md` — per-dimension audit criteria\n2. Read `references/anti-patterns.md` — known evidence anti-patterns and detection\n3. Read `references/harness-verification-trees.md` — module-specific verification protocols",
        "characterCount": 262,
        "estimatedTokens": 66,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#on-load-3"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 4,
        "heading": "Do NOT Load",
        "startLine": 46,
        "endLine": 55,
        "content": "## Do NOT Load\n\nSkip this skill when:\n\n- Performing spec alignment checks — use `gate-spec-compliance` instead\n- Checking lifecycle coverage — use `gate-lifecycle-integration` instead\n- Running research or exploration workflows (not gate verification)\n- The calling workflow has not passed `gate-spec-compliance` yet\n- No implementation artifacts exist to evaluate",
        "characterCount": 364,
        "estimatedTokens": 91,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#do-not-load-4"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 5,
        "heading": "Evidence Hierarchy",
        "startLine": 56,
        "endLine": 69,
        "content": "## Evidence Hierarchy\n\nClassify every evidence artifact into exactly one level:\n\n| Level | Source | Example |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| **L1** | Live OpenCode runtime session | Feature exercised in actual OpenCode instance |\n| **L2** | Continuity record from live run | Delegation/session record captured from real execution |\n| **L3** | Integration test passing | Tests hitting real SDK boundaries (not mocked) |\n| **L4** | Unit test passing | Tests that mock SDK boundaries |\n| **L5** | Documentation/historical summary | Specs, design docs, conversation summaries |\n\n**L5 alone never passes any gate.**",
        "characterCount": 610,
        "estimatedTokens": 153,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#evidence-hierarchy-5"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 6,
        "heading": "Gate Type → Minimum Evidence Level",
        "startLine": 70,
        "endLine": 79,
        "content": "## Gate Type → Minimum Evidence Level\n\n| Gate Type | Minimum Level | Required Evidence |\n|-----------|---------------|-------------------|\n| PR review | L3 | At least integration tests hitting real boundaries |\n| Phase completion | L2 | Continuity record from real run |\n| Merge | L2 | Continuity record from real run |\n| Milestone completion | L2 + one L1 | Continuity record plus at least one live session proof |\n| Release/deployment | L1 | Live runtime proof required |",
        "characterCount": 473,
        "estimatedTokens": 119,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#gate-type-minimum-evidence-level-6"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 7,
        "heading": "Contextual Perspective Activation",
        "startLine": 80,
        "endLine": 93,
        "content": "## Contextual Perspective Activation\n\nDetermine the current context, then activate the appropriate evaluation lens:\n\n| Context | Primary Lens | Secondary Lens | Emphasis |\n|---------|-------------|----------------|---------|\n| Code review | Dev | Architect | Test integrity, mock coverage |\n| Phase audit | Architect | PM | Evidence hierarchy compliance, regression |\n| Milestone verification | PM | Architect | Runtime proof, completion honesty |\n| Integration check | Architect | Dev | Live verification, cross-boundary testing |\n| Deployment readiness | All three | — | Full evidence verification across all dimensions |\n\nLoad `references/perspective-rubrics.md` for per-lens evaluation criteria.",
        "characterCount": 699,
        "estimatedTokens": 175,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#contextual-perspective-activation-7"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 8,
        "heading": "Core Evaluation Workflow",
        "startLine": 94,
        "endLine": 108,
        "content": "## Core Evaluation Workflow\n\nFollow this checklist in order:\n\n```\n- [ ] STEP 1: GATHER — Collect all evidence artifacts for the claim\n- [ ] STEP 2: CLASSIFY — Assign L1–L5 to each artifact using evidence hierarchy\n- [ ] STEP 3: CHECK MINIMUM — Does highest evidence level meet gate-type minimum?\n- [ ] STEP 4: DETECT MOCKS — Any mock-only claims for integration surfaces?\n- [ ] STEP 5: CHECK COMPLETION HONESTY — Any completion claims without backing evidence?\n- [ ] STEP 6: REGRESSION CHECK — Any cross-phase regression risk from dependency graph?\n- [ ] STEP 7: ANTI-PATTERN SCAN — Match evidence against anti-pattern catalog\n- [ ] STEP 8: VERDICT — Render PASS or FAIL with remediation plan\n```",
        "characterCount": 696,
        "estimatedTokens": 174,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#core-evaluation-workflow-8"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 9,
        "heading": "Core Evaluation Workflow",
        "startLine": 109,
        "endLine": 110,
        "content": "See `references/evaluation-workflow-detail.md` for detailed step procedures, mock detection commands, and classification guidance.",
        "characterCount": 130,
        "estimatedTokens": 33,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#core-evaluation-workflow-9"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 10,
        "heading": "Cross-Skill Routing",
        "startLine": 111,
        "endLine": 112,
        "content": "## Cross-Skill Routing",
        "characterCount": 22,
        "estimatedTokens": 6,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#cross-skill-routing-10"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 11,
        "heading": "Triad Flow (lifecycle → spec → evidence)",
        "startLine": 113,
        "endLine": 127,
        "content": "### Triad Flow (lifecycle → spec → evidence)\n\n```\ngate-lifecycle-integration (ENTRY — checks lifecycle correctness)\n    ↓ PASS\ngate-spec-compliance (MIDDLE — checks spec alignment)\n    ↓ PASS\ngate-evidence-truth ← YOU ARE HERE (TERMINAL)\n    ↓\n    ├─ PASS → ALL 3 GATES CLEAR → proceed to merge/release\n    └─ FAIL → STOP → produce remediation plan → route to appropriate fix skill\n```\n\nTriad backward references, evidence collection handoff from `hm-production-readiness`, and full remediation routing are in `references/cross-skill-routing-detail.md`.",
        "characterCount": 553,
        "estimatedTokens": 139,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#triad-flow-lifecycle-spec-evidence-11"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 12,
        "heading": "Related Skills",
        "startLine": 128,
        "endLine": 138,
        "content": "### Related Skills\n\n| Related Skill | Role | Interaction |\n|---------------|------|-------------|\n| `gate-lifecycle-integration` | Entry gate | Must pass before spec compliance runs |\n| `gate-spec-compliance` | Middle gate | Routes to this gate on PASS |\n| `hm-production-readiness` | Evidence collector | Feeds structured L1-L5 evidence into this gate |\n| `hm-cross-cutting-change` | Mock honesty | Remediation target for mock-only failures |\n| `hm-debug` | Root-cause investigation | Remediation for insufficient evidence level |\n| `hm-completion-looping` | Loop-back control | Uses this gate's verdict for loop-back decisions |\n| `hm-coordinating-loop` | Orchestration re-dispatch | Re-dispatches when evidence or regression gaps found |",
        "characterCount": 740,
        "estimatedTokens": 185,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#related-skills-12"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",

        "startLine": 139,
        "endLine": 140,
        "content": "| `hm-gate-orchestrator` | Triad lifecycle manager | Orchestrates triad sequencing, state persistence, cross-gate handoff |",
        "characterCount": 123,
        "estimatedTokens": 31,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#related-skills-13"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 14,
        "heading": "Adopted Patterns",
        "startLine": 141,
        "endLine": 144,
        "content": "## Adopted Patterns\n\nSynthesizes three proven methodologies: **Anthropic Gather→Act→Verify**, **Google Testing Pyramid (70-20-10)**, **GRADE Evidence Framework**. See `references/adopted-patterns.md` for full synthesis.",
        "characterCount": 219,
        "estimatedTokens": 55,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#adopted-patterns-14"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 15,
        "heading": "Self-Correction",
        "startLine": 145,
        "endLine": 146,
        "content": "## Self-Correction",
        "characterCount": 18,
        "estimatedTokens": 5,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#self-correction-15"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 16,
        "heading": "Mode 1: When No Evidence Exists",
        "startLine": 147,
        "endLine": 150,
        "content": "### Mode 1: When No Evidence Exists\n\nDo not downgrade the gate minimum. Report FAIL with explicit instructions: \"Produce [L2/L1] evidence by [running integration test / capturing live session / verifying in OpenCode instance].\"",
        "characterCount": 227,
        "estimatedTokens": 57,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#mode-1-when-no-evidence-exists-16"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 17,
        "heading": "Mode 2: When Evidence Is Ambiguous",
        "startLine": 151,
        "endLine": 154,
        "content": "### Mode 2: When Evidence Is Ambiguous\n\nClassify at the lower level. A test that mocks some but not all SDK boundaries is L4, not L3. A continuity record that may or may not be from a live session is L5, not L2.",
        "characterCount": 211,
        "estimatedTokens": 53,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#mode-2-when-evidence-is-ambiguous-17"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 18,
        "heading": "Mode 3: When the User Overrides",
        "startLine": 155,
        "endLine": 158,
        "content": "### Mode 3: When the User Overrides\n\nDocument the waiver in the evidence report with: which gate failed, what evidence was missing, and the user's rationale. The override must be explicit — never silently waive.",
        "characterCount": 211,
        "estimatedTokens": 53,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#mode-3-when-the-user-overrides-18"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 19,
        "heading": "Mode 4: When Evidence Contradicts Itself",
        "startLine": 159,
        "endLine": 162,
        "content": "### Mode 4: When Evidence Contradicts Itself\n\nIf two artifacts provide conflicting evidence for the same claim (e.g., a passing test but a failing continuity record), treat the contradiction as a regression signal. Classify both artifacts at their individual levels, mark the claim as UNVERIFIED, and require reconciliation before rendering PASS. Never average or weight conflicting evidence — resolve the contradiction or FAIL.",
        "characterCount": 428,
        "estimatedTokens": 107,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#mode-4-when-evidence-contradicts-itself-19"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md",
        "index": 20,
        "heading": "Gate Orchestrator Integration",
        "startLine": 163,
        "endLine": 166,
        "content": "## Gate Orchestrator Integration\n\nThis gate participates in the triad orchestrated by `hm-gate-orchestrator`. The orchestrator manages triad sequencing, state persistence, and cross-gate handoff. When invoked within an orchestrator workflow, this skill receives a pre-built evidence bundle and returns a structured verdict. See `hm-gate-orchestrator` for full triad lifecycle management.",
        "characterCount": 387,
        "estimatedTokens": 97,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-evidence-truth/SKILL.md#gate-orchestrator-integration-20"
      },
      {

        "content": "## Reference Files\n\n| File | Content |\n|------|---------|\n| `references/evaluation-checklist.md` | Per-dimension audit criteria |\n| `references/evaluation-workflow-detail.md` | Detailed STEPS 1-8 procedures and detection commands |\n| `references/cross-skill-routing-detail.md` | Triad backward refs, evidence handoff, remediation routing |\n| `references/perspective-rubrics.md` | PM/Architect/Dev rubrics with contextual activation |\n| `references/anti-patterns.md` | Full anti-pattern catalog with severity and detection |\n| `references/adopted-patterns.md` | Synthesized patterns from Anthropic, Google, GRADE |\n| `references/harness-verification-trees.md` | Module-specific verification trees |\n| `templates/evidence-report.md` | Standardized report template |",
        "characterCount": 763,

{
```

```json
    "action": "read",
```

**Tool: skill**

**Input:**
```json
{
  "name": "gate-l3-lifecycle-integration"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<skill_content name="gate-l3-lifecycle-integration">
# Skill: gate-l3-lifecycle-integration

# Gate: Lifecycle Integration

Internal quality gate for Hivemind harness architecture compliance. Not for end-user
shipping. Activates during every gatekeeping workflow related to harness or OpenCode
integration work.

## Activation Detection

Load this skill when:
- Code review of files in `src/` (any subdirectory)
- Phase audit or milestone verification touching harness modules
- Integration check after tool/hook/delegation changes
- Deployment readiness for `opencode-harness` npm package
- Any workflow referencing `gate-lifecycle-integration`

## Do NOT Load

Skip when working on `.opencode/` soft meta-concept authoring, end-user feature
development, non-Hivemind code reviews, documentation, or artifacts in
`src/shared/`/`src/schema-kernel/` (use lighter classification check instead).

## Two-Halves Classification (Q6)

Every artifact must land in exactly one of three roots:

| Root | Contents | State Authority |
|------|----------|-----------------|
| `src/` (Hard Harness) | Tools, hooks, plugin, shared, lib | Writes to `.hivemind/` via managers only |
| `.opencode/` (Soft Meta-Concepts) | Skills, agents, commands, rules | No persistent state — OpenCode primitives only |
| `.hivemind/` (Deep Module State) | Journals, continuity, delegation records | Canonical state root (Q6) |

Cross-contamination between roots is a **BLOCK** finding. Tools never write to
`.opencode/`. Hooks never write to `.hivemind/` directly — they route through
`DelegationManager` or `continuity.ts`.

## 9-Surface Mutation Authority

The architecture defines 9 surfaces across write-side (4), read-side (3), and
assembly (1). Each artifact must conform to its surface's authority boundaries.

> **Full table with constraints**: `references/nine-surface-authority.md`
>
> Source: `.planning/codebase/ARCHITECTURE.md` § "9-Surface Mutation Authority"

Quick summary — write-side: `continuity.ts`, `delegation-persistence.ts`,
`session-journal.ts`, `DelegationManager`. Read-side: hooks, tools, sidecar.
Assembly: `plugin.ts`.

## OpenCode SDK Surface Compliance

Validate against the real `@opencode-ai/plugin` v1.14.28 API surface. Three areas:

1. **tool() factory**: `description`, `args` (Zod), `execute` → string. Registered in plugin.ts.
2. **Hook handlers**: Exactly 4 hooks (`tool.execute.before/after`, `experimental.session.compacting`, `shell.env`).
3. **Plugin composition**: Async function, type-only imports, no inline business logic, lazy PTY.

> **Full checklists with real signatures**: `references/sdk-compliance.md`
>
> Additional context: `stack-opencode` skill for broader SDK reference.

## CQRS Boundary Enforcement

Write-side (tools) mutate state via managers. Read-side (hooks) observe events.
Events flow write→read, never reverse. 7 BLOCK-level anti-patterns detect violations
(e.g., `AP-WRITE-FROM-READ`, `AP-CROSS-ROOT-WRITE`, `AP-BYPASS-MANAGER`).

> **Full write/read checklists + anti-pattern table**: `references/cqrs-boundaries.md`
>
> Expanded anti-pattern catalog: `references/anti-patterns.md`

## Delegation Hierarchy Constraints

Validate against runtime constants from `src/lib/types.ts`:

| Constant | Value | Meaning |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH` | 3 | Max nested delegation depth |
| `MAX_DESCENDANTS_PER_ROOT` | 10 | Max child delegations per root session |
| `STABLE_POLLS_REQUIRED` | 3 | Consecutive unchanged polls for completion |
| `TASK_CLEANUP_DELAY_MS` | 600000 | Grace period before cleanup (10 min) |

Check: uses `DelegationManager.dispatch()`, valid category, depth ≤ 3, WaiterModel
dispatch, dual-signal completion, recovery guarantee via `recoverPending()`.

## Decision Tree

```
START → Classify the artifact by file location:
  ├─ src/tools/*.ts → TOOL: tool() registration, Zod, response envelope,
  │     SDK mutation via session-api.ts, state via continuity.ts, LOC < 200
  ├─ src/hooks/*.ts → HOOK: factory pattern, real SDK signature, CQRS readonly
  ├─ src/lib/*.ts → LIBRARY: dependency ≤ 2 levels, LOC < 500, no `any`, tests
  ├─ src/plugin.ts → COMPOSITION: LOC < 200, all registered, no inline logic
  ├─ src/shared/*.ts, src/schema-kernel/*.ts → LEAF: cross-cutting utility
  └─ DELEGATION participant? → DelegationManager, category, depth, dual-signal
```

For each branch, execute the detailed checklist in `references/evaluation-checklist.md`.

## Self-Correction

### Mode 1: When Classification Is Ambiguous

If a file straddles two classification roots (e.g., a test helper in `src/shared/` that also reads `.hivemind/` state), classify by primary purpose. Helpers that are pure utility = LEAF (src/shared/). Helpers that read persistent state = suspect — check if they route through a manager. Never classify as LEAF to bypass the lifecycle gate.

### Mode 2: When CQRS Boundary Is Fuzzy

Some tools produce side effects that look like state reads (e.g., delegation-status.ts reads delegation records). This is correct CQRS: the tool reads through a query API, not by subscribing to events. Distinguish: direct event subscription in a tool = BLOCK; query API call in a tool = PASS.

### Mode 3: When Plugin.ts Exceeds LOC Limit

If plugin.ts exceeds 200 LOC but the excess is purely registration boilerplate (many tools/hooks), document the finding as WARNING rather than BLOCK. If the excess includes inline business logic, BLOCK. Registration-only LOC inflation is acceptable; logic inflation is not.

### Mode 4: When Delegation Depth Is At Limit

A delegation chain at exactly MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH (3) is PASS — the limit is inclusive. However, if depth=3 and the chain also uses queue keys without buildDelegationQueueKey(), flag as WARNING. At-limit depth with correct queue key construction = PASS. At-limit depth with manual key construction = WARNING (fragile).

## Gate Orchestrator Integration

This gate participates in the triad orchestrated by `hm-gate-orchestrator`. The orchestrator manages triad sequencing, state persistence, and cross-gate handoff. When invoked within an orchestrator workflow, this skill is the ENTRY gate. It receives a gate context from the orchestrator and returns a structured lifecycle verdict. See `hm-gate-orchestrator` for full triad lifecycle management.

## Cross-Skill Routing

- **PASSES** → Route to `gate-spec-compliance` (spec-level verification)
- **FAILS (classification)** → STOP. Redesign required — root misplacement needs file move.
- **FAILS (other)** → Document in gate report, fix, re-run before routing to `gate-spec-compliance`.

### Triad Flow

```
gate-lifecycle-integration  →  gate-spec-compliance  →  gate-evidence-truth
  (entry — this skill)          (spec verification)       (terminal — evidence)
```

## Remediation Routing (on FAIL)

| Finding Type | Route To | Action |
|-------------|----------|--------|
| Classification violation | `hm-coordinating-loop` | Move file to correct root |
| Lifecycle wiring issue | `hm-phase-execution` | Fix registration wiring |
| Structural/architectural | `hm-refactor` | Split module, break cycle |
| CQRS boundary violation | `hm-phase-execution` | Fix CQRS wiring |
| Delegation hierarchy | `hm-coordinating-loop` | Redesign dispatch patterns |
| Unknown/unclear failure | `hm-debug` | Root-cause investigation |
| Completion verification | `hm-completion-looping` | Verification loop |
| Triad orchestration | `hm-gate-orchestrator` | Full triad lifecycle, state persistence, re-run |

> Full routing table: `references/remediation-paths.md`

## Evaluation Output

1. Fill `templates/gate-report.md` with findings per dimension
2. Record PASS/FAIL per anti-pattern check
3. Record which 9-surface authority boundary was checked
4. If PASS: note routing to `gate-spec-compliance`
5. If FAIL: list remediations with file:line references and routing target

## Bundled Resources

| Resource | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `references/evaluation-checklist.md` | Per-artifact-type audit criteria |
| `references/perspective-rubrics.md` | PM/Architect/Dev scoring rubrics |
| `references/anti-patterns.md` | Full anti-pattern catalog |
| `references/adopted-patterns.md` | Synthesized third-party patterns |
| `references/remediation-paths.md` | Per-finding routing to hm-* skills |
| `references/nine-surface-authority.md` | Full 9-surface mutation authority table |
| `references/sdk-compliance.md` | OpenCode SDK compliance checklists |
| `references/cqrs-boundaries.md` | CQRS boundary rules + BLOCK anti-patterns |
| `references/gap-documentation.md` | Full gap catalog |
| `references/triad-flow.md` | Inter-gate handoff contracts |
| `metrics/rich-gate-scorecard.md` | RICH-8 scorecard |
| `evals/evals.json` | Test scenarios |
| `templates/gate-report.md` | Standardized report template |
| `scripts/run-gate-eval.sh` | Deterministic evaluation runner |
```

        "endLine": 63,
        "content": "## Two-Halves Classification (Q6)\n\nEvery artifact must land in exactly one of three roots:\n\n| Root | Contents | State Authority |\n|------|----------|-----------------|\n| `src/` (Hard Harness) | Tools, hooks, plugin, shared, lib | Writes to `.hivemind/` via managers only |\n| `.opencode/` (Soft Meta-Concepts) | Skills, agents, commands, rules | No persistent state — OpenCode primitives only |",

        "startLine": 95,
        "endLine": 104,
        "content": "## CQRS Boundary Enforcement\n\nWrite-side (tools) mutate state via managers. Read-side (hooks) observe events.\nEvents flow write→read, never reverse. 7 BLOCK-level anti-patterns detect violations\n(e.g., `AP-WRITE-FROM-READ`, `AP-CROSS-ROOT-WRITE`, `AP-BYPASS-MANAGER`).\n\n> **Full write/read checklists + anti-pattern table**: `references/cqrs-boundaries.md`\n>\n> Expanded anti-pattern catalog: `references/anti-patterns.md`",
        "characterCount": 421,
        "estimatedTokens": 106,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#cqrs-boundary-enforcement-10"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md",
        "index": 11,
        "heading": "Delegation Hierarchy Constraints",
        "startLine": 105,
        "endLine": 115,
        "content": "## Delegation Hierarchy Constraints\n\nValidate against runtime constants from `src/lib/types.ts`:\n\n| Constant | Value | Meaning |\n|----------|-------|---------|\n| `MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH` | 3 | Max nested delegation depth |\n| `MAX_DESCENDANTS_PER_ROOT` | 10 | Max child delegations per root session |\n| `STABLE_POLLS_REQUIRED` | 3 | Consecutive unchanged polls for completion |\n| `TASK_CLEANUP_DELAY_MS` | 600000 | Grace period before cleanup (10 min) |",
        "characterCount": 450,
        "estimatedTokens": 113,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#delegation-hierarchy-constraints-11"

        "index": 12,
        "heading": "Delegation Hierarchy Constraints",
        "startLine": 116,
        "endLine": 118,
        "content": "Check: uses `DelegationManager.dispatch()`, valid category, depth ≤ 3, WaiterModel\ndispatch, dual-signal completion, recovery guarantee via `recoverPending()`.",
        "characterCount": 159,
        "estimatedTokens": 40,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#delegation-hierarchy-constraints-12"

      {

        "index": 13,
        "heading": "Decision Tree",
        "startLine": 119,
        "endLine": 127,
        "content": "## Decision Tree\n\n```\nSTART → Classify the artifact by file location:\n  ├─ src/tools/*.ts → TOOL: tool() registration, Zod, response envelope,\n  │     SDK mutation via session-api.ts, state via continuity.ts, LOC < 200\n  ├─ src/hooks/*.ts → HOOK: factory pattern, real SDK signature, CQRS readonly\n  ├─ src/lib/*.ts → LIBRARY: dependency ≤ 2 levels, LOC < 500, no `any`, tests\n  ├─ src/plugin.ts → COMPOSITION: LOC < 200, all registered, no inline logic",
        "characterCount": 453,
        "estimatedTokens": 114,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#decision-tree-13"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md",
        "index": 14,
        "heading": "Decision Tree",
        "startLine": 128,
        "endLine": 133,
        "content": "├─ src/shared/*.ts, src/schema-kernel/*.ts → LEAF: cross-cutting utility\n  └─ DELEGATION participant? → DelegationManager, category, depth, dual-signal\n```\n\nFor each branch, execute the detailed checklist in `references/evaluation-checklist.md`.",
        "characterCount": 245,
        "estimatedTokens": 62,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#decision-tree-14"
      },

        "index": 16,
        "heading": "Mode 1: When Classification Is Ambiguous",
        "startLine": 136,
        "endLine": 139,
        "content": "### Mode 1: When Classification Is Ambiguous\n\nIf a file straddles two classification roots (e.g., a test helper in `src/shared/` that also reads `.hivemind/` state), classify by primary purpose. Helpers that are pure utility = LEAF (src/shared/). Helpers that read persistent state = suspect — check if they route through a manager. Never classify as LEAF to bypass the lifecycle gate.",
        "characterCount": 385,
        "estimatedTokens": 97,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#mode-1-when-classification-is-ambiguous-16"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md",
        "index": 17,
        "heading": "Mode 2: When CQRS Boundary Is Fuzzy",
        "startLine": 140,
        "endLine": 143,
        "content": "### Mode 2: When CQRS Boundary Is Fuzzy\n\nSome tools produce side effects that look like state reads (e.g., delegation-status.ts reads delegation records). This is correct CQRS: the tool reads through a query API, not by subscribing to events. Distinguish: direct event subscription in a tool = BLOCK; query API call in a tool = PASS.",
        "characterCount": 333,
        "estimatedTokens": 84,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#mode-2-when-cqrs-boundary-is-fuzzy-17"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md",
        "index": 18,
        "heading": "Mode 3: When Plugin.ts Exceeds LOC Limit",
        "startLine": 144,
        "endLine": 147,
        "content": "### Mode 3: When Plugin.ts Exceeds LOC Limit\n\nIf plugin.ts exceeds 200 LOC but the excess is purely registration boilerplate (many tools/hooks), document the finding as WARNING rather than BLOCK. If the excess includes inline business logic, BLOCK. Registration-only LOC inflation is acceptable; logic inflation is not.",
        "characterCount": 319,
        "estimatedTokens": 80,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md#mode-3-when-plugints-exceeds-loc-limit-18"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/gate-l3-lifecycle-integration/SKILL.md",
        "index": 19,
        "heading": "Mode 4: When Delegation Depth Is At Limit",
        "startLine": 148,
        "endLine": 151,
        "content": "### Mode 4: When Delegation Depth Is At Limit\n\nA delegation chain at exactly MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH (3) is PASS — the limit is inclusive. However, if depth=3 and the chain also uses queue keys without buildDelegationQueueKey(), flag as WARNING. At-limit depth with correct queue key construction = PASS. At-limit depth with manual key construction = WARNING (fragile).",
        "characterCount": 366,
        "estimatedTokens": 92,

        "content": "## Gate Orchestrator Integration\n\nThis gate participates in the triad orchestrated by `hm-gate-orchestrator`.         "content": "## Overview\n\nThis reference skill documents ALL tools available in the Hivemind ecosystem across three categories: native OpenCode built-in tools, Hivemind custom harness tools (from `opencode-harness` npm package), and MCP-provided external tools. It provides per-depth typical permission patterns derived from the actual 56 agent definitions in `.opencode/agents/`, and documents lineage-specific tool rules.\n\n**Purpose:** When configuring agent permissions, designing delegation boundaries, or auditing tool exposure, use this matrix to make consistent, lineage-compliant decisions.",

```
        "content": "## On Load\n\n1. Identify the agent depth (L0/L1/L2/L3) and lineage (hm/hf/gate/stack/unprefixed)\n2. Scan the per-depth matrix for the agent's typical tool allowances\n3. Check the per-lineage rules for cross-lineage restrictions\n4. If designing a new agent, use the `Per-Depth Typical Permissions` section as a starting template\n5. Verify any proposed tool allowances against the lineage rules before finalizing\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 414,

        "heading": "1A. Native OpenCode Tools",
        "startLine": 55,
        "endLine": 62,
        "content": "### 1A. Native OpenCode Tools\n\nThese are built into every OpenCode runtime. Always available unless explicitly denied.\n\n| Tool | Category | Permissions | Glob/Pattern Support | Description |\n|------|----------|------------|----------------------|-------------|\n| `read` | Read | allow/ask/ask | Yes (file path) | Read file contents. Supports line ranges. `.env` files denied by default. |\n| `grep` | Read | allow/ask/ask | Yes (regex) | Search file contents using regex. Respects `.gitignore` via ripgrep. |",
        "characterCount": 509,
        "estimatedTokens": 128,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1a-native-opencode-tools-7"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 8,
        "heading": "1A. Native OpenCode Tools",
        "startLine": 63,
        "endLine": 66,
        "content": "| `glob` | Read | allow/ask/ask | Yes (glob patterns) | Find files by pattern matching. Returns paths sorted by modification time. |\n| `list` | Read | allow/ask/ask | Yes (directory path) | List files and directories. Supports glob filtering. |\n| `edit` | Write | allow/ask/ask | Yes (file path) | Modify existing files via exact string replacements. Covers `write`, `patch`, `multiedit`. |\n| `write` | Write | allow/ask/ask | Yes (file path) | Create new files or overwrite existing ones. Controlled by `edit` permission. |",
        "characterCount": 528,
        "estimatedTokens": 132,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1a-native-opencode-tools-8"

        "startLine": 67,
        "endLine": 70,
        "content": "| `patch` | Write | allow/ask/ask | — | Apply patches/diffs to codebase. Controlled by `edit` permission. |\n| `bash` | Execute | allow/ask/ask | Yes (command prefix) | Execute shell commands. Granular rules match command prefixes (e.g., `git *`). |\n| `task` | Dispatch | allow/ask/ask | Yes (subagent type) | Launch subagent sessions. Disabled for subagents by default. |\n| `skill` | Load | allow/ask/ask | Yes (skill name) | Load SKILL.md content. Pattern matching by skill name. |",
        "characterCount": 486,
        "estimatedTokens": 122,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1a-native-opencode-tools-9"

        "endLine": 75,
        "content": "| `todowrite` | Tracking | allow/ask/ask | — | Manage structured task lists. Disabled for subagents by default. |\n| `webfetch` | Network | allow/ask/ask | Yes (URL) | Fetch web page content as markdown. Pattern matching by URL. |\n| `websearch` | Network | allow/ask/ask | Yes (query) | Search the web via Exa AI. Requires `OPENCODE_ENABLE_EXA=1`. |\n| `codesearch` | Network | allow/ask/ask | Yes (query) | Search code across repositories. |\n| `question` | Interaction | allow/ask/ask | — | Ask user questions during execution with structured options. |",
        "characterCount": 557,
        "estimatedTokens": 140,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1a-native-opencode-tools-10"

        "endLine": 82,
        "content": "| `lsp` | Intelligence | allow/ask/ask | — (experimental) | Interact with LSP servers: definitions, references, hover, call hierarchy. Requires `OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_LSP_TOOL=true`. |\n\n**Permission special cases:**\n- `external_directory` — triggered when any tool touches paths outside the working directory. Default: `ask`.\n- `doom_loop` — triggered when the same tool call repeats 3+ times with identical input. Default: `ask`.\n- `edit` covers ALL file modification tools: `edit`, `write`, `patch`, `multiedit`.",
        "characterCount": 515,
        "estimatedTokens": 129,

        "endLine": 89,
        "content": "### 1B. Hivemind Custom Tools (opencode-harness)\n\nThese are registered by the `opencode-harness` npm package plugin. Available when the plugin is loaded in `opencode.json`.\n\n| Tool | Source File | Category | Permissions | Description |\n|------|-----------|----------|------------|-------------|\n| `delegate-task` | `src/tools/delegate-task.ts` | Delegation | allow/ask/ask | Delegate work to a specialist agent via SDK child-session dispatch. Always-background WaiterModel. Returns delegation ID immediately. |",
        "characterCount": 511,
        "estimatedTokens": 128,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1b-hivemind-custom-tools-opencode-harness-12"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 13,
        "heading": "1B. Hivemind Custom Tools (opencode-harness)",
        "startLine": 90,
        "endLine": 91,
        "content": "| `delegation-status` | `src/tools/delegation-status.ts` | Delegation | allow/ask/ask | Check delegation status and retrieve results. Query by delegation ID or filter by status. |\n| `run-background-command` | `src/tools/run-background-command.ts` | Execution | allow/ask/ask | Run CLI commands in shared background PTY sessions with queue-governed dispatch, output reads, interactive input, and termination. |",
        "characterCount": 411,
        "estimatedTokens": 103,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1b-hivemind-custom-tools-opencode-harness-13"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 14,
        "heading": "1B. Hivemind Custom Tools (opencode-harness)",
        "startLine": 92,
        "endLine": 94,
        "content": "| `session-journal-export` | `src/tools/session-journal-export.ts` | Persistence | allow/ask/ask | Export bounded Session Journal and Execution Lineage quick-read summaries as JSON or Markdown. |\n| `prompt-skim` | `src/tools/prompt-skim/` | Analysis | allow/ask/ask | Fast scan of prompt content: count words/lines/tokens, extract URLs, verify file paths, calculate complexity score. |\n| `prompt-analyze` | `src/tools/prompt-analyze/` | Analysis | allow/ask/ask | Deep prompt analysis: contradictions, vagueness, missing scope, clarity signals. |",
        "characterCount": 549,
        "estimatedTokens": 138,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1b-hivemind-custom-tools-opencode-harness-14"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 15,
        "heading": "1B. Hivemind Custom Tools (opencode-harness)",
        "startLine": 95,
        "endLine": 96,
        "content": "| `session-patch` | `src/tools/session-patch/` | Mutation | allow/ask/ask | Patch specific sections in a session file with backup. |\n| `configure-primitive` | `src/tools/configure-primitive.ts` | Configuration | allow/ask/ask | Configure, read, list, or inspect OpenCode primitives (agent, command, skill). Supports compile/decompile from JSON/YAML with dry-run preview. |",
        "characterCount": 374,
        "estimatedTokens": 94,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1b-hivemind-custom-tools-opencode-harness-15"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 16,
        "heading": "1B. Hivemind Custom Tools (opencode-harness)",
        "startLine": 97,
        "endLine": 99,
        "content": "| `validate-restart` | `src/tools/validate-restart.ts` | Validation | allow/ask/ask | Validate that compiled OpenCode primitives are discoverable and free of runtime issues after restart. Checks circular deps, missing refs, permission inheritance breaks. |\n| `nl-route` | (harness-native) | Routing | allow/ask/ask | Route a natural language request to one of three test commands (test-echo, test-list, test-status). |",
        "characterCount": 420,
        "estimatedTokens": 105,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1b-hivemind-custom-tools-opencode-harness-16"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 17,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 100,
        "endLine": 106,
        "content": "### 1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)\n\nThese tools are available when their MCP servers are configured in `opencode.json`. Availability depends on API keys and server configuration.\n\n| Provider | Key Tools | Category | Auth Required |\n|----------|-----------|----------|---------------|\n| **Brave Search** | `brave_web_search`, `brave_local_search`, `brave_video_search`, `brave_image_search`, `brave_news_search`, `brave_summarizer` | Web | API key (`BRAVE_API_KEY`) |",
        "characterCount": 479,
        "estimatedTokens": 120,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-17"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 18,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 107,
        "endLine": 110,
        "content": "| **Tavily** | `tavily_search`, `tavily_extract`, `tavily_crawl`, `tavily_map`, `tavily_research` | Web | API key (`TAVILY_API_KEY`) |\n| **Exa** | `exa_web_search_exa`, `exa_web_fetch_exa` | Web | API key (`EXA_API_KEY`) |\n| **Context7** | `context7_resolve-library-id`, `context7_query-docs` | Docs | API key or anonymous |\n| **DeepWiki** | `deepwiki_read_wiki_structure`, `deepwiki_read_wiki_contents`, `deepwiki_ask_question` | Docs | None |",
        "characterCount": 444,
        "estimatedTokens": 111,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-18"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 19,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 111,
        "endLine": 111,
        "content": "| **Repomix** | `repomix_pack_codebase`, `repomix_pack_remote_repository`, `repomix_generate_skill`, `repomix_attach_packed_output`, `repomix_read_repomix_output`, `repomix_grep_repomix_output` | Code | None |",
        "characterCount": 209,
        "estimatedTokens": 53,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-19"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 20,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 112,
        "endLine": 112,
        "content": "| **GitHub** | `github_create_or_update_file`, `github_search_repositories`, `github_create_repository`, `github_get_file_contents`, `github_push_files`, `github_create_issue`, `github_create_pull_request`, `github_fork_repository`, `github_create_branch`, `github_list_commits`, `github_list_issues`, `github_update_issue`, `github_add_issue_comment`, `github_search_code`, `github_search_issues`, `github_search_users`, `github_get_issue`, `github_get_pull_request`, `github_list_pull_requests`, `github_create_pull_request_review`, `github_merge_pull_request`, `github_get_pull_request_files`, `github_get_pull_request_status`, `github_update_pull_request_branch`, `github_get_pull_request_comments`, `github_get_pull_request_reviews` | Git | `GITHUB_TOKEN` |",
        "characterCount": 762,
        "estimatedTokens": 191,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-20"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 21,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 113,
        "endLine": 113,
        "content": "| **GitMCP** | `gitmcp_fetch_github_com_documentation`, `gitmcp_search_github_com_documentation`, `gitmcp_search_github_com_code`, `gitmcp_fetch_generic_url_content` | Git | None |",
        "characterCount": 180,
        "estimatedTokens": 45,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-21"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 22,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 114,
        "endLine": 115,
        "content": "| **Playwright** | `mcp_playwright_browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_snapshot`, `browser_screenshot`, `browser_fill_form`, `browser_type`, `browser_evaluate`, `browser_tabs`, `browser_wait_for`, `browser_close`, `browser_resize`, `browser_console_messages`, `browser_handle_dialog`, `browser_file_upload`, `browser_drag`, `browser_hover`, `browser_select_option`, `browser_press_key`, `browser_run_code` | Browser | None (uses bundled Chromium) |\n| **Fetcher** | `fetcher_fetch_url`, `fetcher_fetch_urls`, `fetcher_browser_install` | Web | None |",
        "characterCount": 557,
        "estimatedTokens": 140,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-22"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 23,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 116,
        "endLine": 119,
        "content": "| **Sequential Thinking** | `sequential_thinking_sequentialthinking` | Reasoning | None |\n| **Stitch** | `stitch_create_project`, `stitch_get_project`, `stitch_list_projects`, `stitch_list_screens`, `stitch_get_screen`, `stitch_generate_screen_from_text`, `stitch_edit_screens`, `stitch_generate_variants`, `stitch_create_design_system`, `stitch_update_design_system`, `stitch_list_design_systems`, `stitch_apply_design_system` | Design | API key |\n| **MCP Playwright (alt)** | `mcp-playwright_browser_navigate`, etc. | Browser | None |",
        "characterCount": 536,
        "estimatedTokens": 134,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-23"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 24,
        "heading": "1C. MCP-Provided Tools (External Providers)",
        "startLine": 120,
        "endLine": 121,
        "content": "> **Note:** MCP tool names use the `mcp__server__tool` or `server_tool` format depending on the MCP transport. Actual tool names in your environment may differ. Use `list` or check MCP server configuration for exact names.",
        "characterCount": 222,
        "estimatedTokens": 56,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1c-mcp-provided-tools-external-providers-24"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 25,
        "heading": "1D. Special Permission Guards",
        "startLine": 122,
        "endLine": 131,
        "content": "### 1D. Special Permission Guards\n\n| Guard | Default | When Triggered | Controls |\n|-------|---------|----------------|----------|\n| `external_directory` | `ask` | Any tool touches paths outside working directory | All file-system tools (read, edit, bash, glob, grep, list) |\n| `doom_loop` | `ask` | Same tool call repeats 3+ times with identical input | All tools |\n| `read` → `*.env` | `ask` | Reading `.env` files | `read` tool specifically |\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 451,
        "estimatedTokens": 113,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#1d-special-permission-guards-25"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 26,
        "heading": "Part 2: Per-Depth Typical Permissions",
        "startLine": 132,
        "endLine": 135,
        "content": "## Part 2: Per-Depth Typical Permissions\n\nDerived from the actual 56 agent definitions in `.opencode/agents/`. Use these as starting templates when designing new agents.",
        "characterCount": 169,
        "estimatedTokens": 43,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#part-2-per-depth-typical-permissions-26"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 27,
        "heading": "L0 (Orchestrator — Primary, front-facing)",
        "startLine": 136,
        "endLine": 147,
        "content": "### L0 (Orchestrator — Primary, front-facing)\n\nL0 orchestrators coordinate multi-agent workflows. They route, they do not implement.\n\n| Tool | Typical | Rationale |\n|------|---------|-----------|\n| `read` | `allow` | Read project context, plans, state files |\n| `edit` | `ask` | Orchestrators do not modify files |\n| `write` | `ask` | Orchestrators do not create files |\n| `bash` | `ask` (all) | No shell execution; delegate to L1/L2 |\n| `glob` | `allow` | Discover project structure |\n| `grep` | `allow` | Search codebase for context |",
        "characterCount": 539,
        "estimatedTokens": 135,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l0-orchestrator-primary-front-facing-27"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 28,
        "heading": "L0 (Orchestrator — Primary, front-facing)",
        "startLine": 148,
        "endLine": 154,
        "content": "| `task` | `allow` (specific agents) | Primary function: dispatch L1 coordinators |\n| `delegate-task` | `allow` | Harness delegation with tracking |\n| `delegation-status` | `allow` | Monitor dispatched tasks |\n| `skill` | `allow` | Load coordinator skills |\n\n**Actual L0 agent examples:** `hm-l0-orchestrator`, `hf-l0-orchestrator`, `gsd-executor`",
        "characterCount": 347,
        "estimatedTokens": 87,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l0-orchestrator-primary-front-facing-28"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 29,
        "heading": "L1 (Coordinator — Subagent, dispatches L2 specialists)",
        "startLine": 155,
        "endLine": 166,
        "content": "### L1 (Coordinator — Subagent, dispatches L2 specialists)\n\nL1 coordinators manage parallel task waves. They orchestrate subagent dispatch but do not implement directly.\n\n| Tool | Typical | Rationale |\n|------|---------|-----------|\n| `read` | `allow` | Read context, plans, subagent results |\n| `edit` | `ask` | Coordinators do not modify files |\n| `write` | `ask` | Coordinators do not create files |\n| `bash` | Restricted: `git *`, `node *` | Git operations, Node.js for state queries |\n| `glob` | `allow` | Discover project structure |\n| `grep` | `allow` | Search codebase for context |",
        "characterCount": 592,
        "estimatedTokens": 148,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l1-coordinator-subagent-dispatches-l2-specialists-29"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 30,
        "heading": "L1 (Coordinator — Subagent, dispatches L2 specialists)",
        "startLine": 167,
        "endLine": 173,
        "content": "| `task` | `allow` (all hm-l2-* or hf-l2-* agents) | Primary function: dispatch L2 specialists |\n| `delegate-task` | `allow` or `ask` | Varies — hm coordinators ask, hf coordinators may allow |\n| `delegation-status` | `allow` or `ask` | Varies |\n| `skill` | `allow` | Load coordination skills |\n\n**Actual L1 agent examples:** `hm-l1-coordinator`, `hf-l1-coordinator`",
        "characterCount": 369,
        "estimatedTokens": 93,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l1-coordinator-subagent-dispatches-l2-specialists-30"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 31,
        "heading": "L2 (Specialist — Subagent, implements/analyzes)",
        "startLine": 174,
        "endLine": 177,
        "content": "### L2 (Specialist — Subagent, implements/analyzes)\n\nL2 specialists perform the actual work: research, implementation, review, debugging, authoring. Permissions are domain-specific.",
        "characterCount": 181,
        "estimatedTokens": 46,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l2-specialist-subagent-implementsanalyzes-31"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 32,
        "heading": "Read-Only L2 (researcher, reviewer, auditor, validator, evaluator)",
        "startLine": 178,
        "endLine": 189,
        "content": "#### Read-Only L2 (researcher, reviewer, auditor, validator, evaluator)\n\n| Tool | Typical | Rationale |\n|------|---------|-----------|\n| `read` | `allow` | Core function |\n| `edit` | `ask` | Read-only role |\n| `write` | `ask` | Read-only role |\n| `bash` | Restricted: `git *`, `node *`, `npx *` | Git status, test execution, Node.js CLI tools |\n| `glob` | `allow` | Search and navigation |\n| `grep` | `allow` | Content search |\n| `task` | `ask` (all) or restricted to specific sub-agents | Most read-only specialists cannot delegate |\n| `webfetch` | `allow` | Research tasks |",
        "characterCount": 579,
        "estimatedTokens": 145,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#read-only-l2-researcher-reviewer-auditor-validator-evaluator-32"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 33,
        "heading": "Read-Only L2 (researcher, reviewer, auditor, validator, evaluator)",
        "startLine": 190,
        "endLine": 193,
        "content": "| `websearch` | `allow` | Research tasks |\n\n**Actual read-only L2 agent examples:** `hm-l2-researcher`, `hm-l2-reviewer`, `hm-l2-auditor`, `hm-l2-validator`, `hm-l2-assessor`, `hm-l2-scout`, `hm-l2-synthesizer`",
        "characterCount": 210,
        "estimatedTokens": 53,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#read-only-l2-researcher-reviewer-auditor-validator-evaluator-33"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 34,
        "heading": "Read-Write L2 (executor, fixer, builder)",
        "startLine": 194,
        "endLine": 205,
        "content": "#### Read-Write L2 (executor, fixer, builder)\n\n| Tool | Typical | Rationale |\n|------|---------|-----------|\n| `read` | `allow` | Read source files |\n| `edit` | `allow` | Modify code |\n| `write` | `allow` | Create new files |\n| `bash` | Restricted: `git *`, `node *`, `npm *`, `npx *` | Build, test, commit |\n| `glob` | `allow` | File discovery |\n| `grep` | `allow` | Code search |\n| `task` | `ask` (all) or restricted (e.g., `hm-l2-reviewer`) | Most cannot delegate; some can dispatch reviewers |\n| `webfetch` | `allow` if research needed | Documentation lookup |",
        "characterCount": 565,
        "estimatedTokens": 142,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#read-write-l2-executor-fixer-builder-34"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 35,
        "heading": "Read-Write L2 (executor, fixer, builder)",
        "startLine": 206,
        "endLine": 209,
        "content": "| `websearch` | `allow` if research needed | Internet search |\n\n**Actual read-write L2 agent examples:** `hm-l2-executor`, `hm-l2-debugger`, `hm-l2-implementator`, `hm-l2-optimizer`, `hf-l2-skill-builder`, `hf-l2-agent-builder`, `hf-l2-command-builder`, `hf-l2-tool-builder`",
        "characterCount": 274,
        "estimatedTokens": 69,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#read-write-l2-executor-fixer-builder-35"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 36,
        "heading": "Hivemind Harness Tool Denials at L2",
        "startLine": 210,
        "endLine": 220,
        "content": "#### Hivemind Harness Tool Denials at L2\n\nMost L2 agents explicitly ask these harness tools (they are L0/L1 tools):\n\n| Tool | L2 Default | Rationale |\n|------|-----------|-----------|\n| `delegate-task` | `ask` | Delegation is coordinator privilege |\n| `delegation-status` | `ask` | Status polling is coordinator privilege |\n| `session-journal-export` | `ask` | Session management is coordinator privilege |\n| `prompt-skim` | `ask` | Prompt pipeline is coordinator/service privilege |\n| `prompt-analyze` | `ask` | Prompt pipeline is coordinator/service privilege |",
        "characterCount": 569,
        "estimatedTokens": 143,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#hivemind-harness-tool-denials-at-l2-36"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 37,
        "heading": "Hivemind Harness Tool Denials at L2",
        "startLine": 221,
        "endLine": 225,
        "content": "| `session-patch` | `ask` | Session mutation is coordinator privilege |\n| `configure-primitive` | `ask` | Primitive configuration is meta-builder privilege |\n| `validate-restart` | `ask` | System validation is meta-builder privilege |\n| `run-background-command` | `ask` | Background execution is coordinator privilege |",
        "characterCount": 323,
        "estimatedTokens": 81,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#hivemind-harness-tool-denials-at-l2-37"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 38,
        "heading": "L3 (Reference — Read-only, research/reference-only)",
        "startLine": 226,
        "endLine": 237,
        "content": "### L3 (Reference — Read-only, research/reference-only)\n\nL3 skills are reference and research skills. They are loaded by the `skill` tool, not dispatched as agents. When an L3 skill is activated as an agent:\n\n| Tool | Typical | Rationale |\n|------|---------|-----------|\n| `read` | `allow` | Read reference files |\n| `grep` | `allow` | Search reference content |\n| `glob` | `allow` | Find reference files |\n| `edit` | `ask` | Reference skills do not modify code |\n| `write` | `ask` | Reference skills do not create code |\n| `bash` | `ask` (all) | No execution |",
        "characterCount": 564,
        "estimatedTokens": 141,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l3-reference-read-only-researchreference-only-38"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 39,
        "heading": "L3 (Reference — Read-only, research/reference-only)",
        "startLine": 238,
        "endLine": 242,
        "content": "| `task` | `ask` (all) | No delegation |\n| `webfetch` | `ask` | Not needed for offline reference |\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 105,
        "estimatedTokens": 27,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#l3-reference-read-only-researchreference-only-39"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 40,
        "heading": "Part 3: Per-Lineage Tool Rules",
        "startLine": 243,
        "endLine": 244,
        "content": "## Part 3: Per-Lineage Tool Rules",
        "characterCount": 33,
        "estimatedTokens": 9,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#part-3-per-lineage-tool-rules-40"
      },
      {

        "heading": "hm-* Lineage (Product Development) — STRICT",
        "startLine": 245,
        "endLine": 261,
        "content": "### hm-* Lineage (Product Development) — STRICT\n\n```\nhm-* agents MAY ONLY load:\n  ✅ hm-* skills (product development)\n  ✅ gate-* skills (quality triad)\n  ✅ stack-* skills (tech reference)\n  \nhm-* agents MUST NOT:\n  ❌ hf-* skills (meta-builder) — route to hf-orchestrator instead\n  ❌ hf-* agents (via task or delegate-task)\n  ❌ Run meta-concept creation tools (configure-primitive, validate-restart)\n```\n\n**Tool boundary enforcement at hm-L1/L2:**\n- `skill` tool: only hm-*, gate-*, stack-* skill names allowed\n- `task` tool: only hm-* agent types allowed",
        "characterCount": 554,
        "estimatedTokens": 139,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#hm-lineage-product-development-strict-41"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 42,
        "heading": "hm-* Lineage (Product Development) — STRICT",
        "startLine": 262,
        "endLine": 265,
        "content": "- `delegate-task`: only hm-* agent types allowed\n- `configure-primitive`: always ask\n- `validate-restart`: always ask",
        "characterCount": 119,
        "estimatedTokens": 30,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#hm-lineage-product-development-strict-42"

      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 43,
        "heading": "hf-* Lineage (Meta-Builder) — FLEXIBLE",
        "startLine": 266,
        "endLine": 285,
        "content": "### hf-* Lineage (Meta-Builder) — FLEXIBLE\n\n```\nhf-* agents MAY LOAD:\n  ✅ hf-* skills (meta-builder)\n  ✅ hm-* skills (for codebase investigation, cross-validation)\n  ✅ gate-* skills (quality triad)\n  ✅ stack-* skills (tech reference)\n\nhf-* agents MAY DELEGATE:\n  ✅ hf-* agents (meta-builder specialists)\n  ✅ hm-* agents (for codebase investigation tasks)\n\nhf-* agents have WRITE access to:\n  ✅ .opencode/ (agents, commands, skills, rules, permissions)\n  ✅ src/tools/ (tool implementors)\n  ✅ .hivefiver-meta-builder/ (lab source of truth)\n```\n\n**Tool permissions for hf-L2 agents:**",
        "characterCount": 581,
        "estimatedTokens": 146,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#hf-lineage-meta-builder-flexible-43"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 44,
        "heading": "hf-* Lineage (Meta-Builder) — FLEXIBLE",
        "startLine": 286,
        "endLine": 289,
        "content": "- `edit`/`write`: restricted to `.opencode/**`, `src/tools/**`, `.hivefiver-meta-builder/**`\n- `configure-primitive`: allow (core meta-builder function)\n- `validate-restart`: allow (core meta-builder function)",
        "characterCount": 209,
        "estimatedTokens": 53,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#hf-lineage-meta-builder-flexible-44"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 45,
        "heading": "gate-* Lineage (Internal Quality Gates) — INTERNAL ONLY",
        "startLine": 290,
        "endLine": 304,
        "content": "### gate-* Lineage (Internal Quality Gates) — INTERNAL ONLY\n\n```\ngate-* skills are THIS PROJECT ONLY (not shipped).\nThey are internal quality verification gates.\n\ngate-* skills:\n  ✅ May be loaded by hm-* and hf-* agents (as quality checks)\n  ✅ Run as read-only evaluations\n  ✅ Produce scorecards and evidence reports\n  ❌ May NOT modify code\n  ❌ May NOT delegate to other agents\n  ❌ Are NOT included in shipped npm package\n```",
        "characterCount": 425,
        "estimatedTokens": 107,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#gate-lineage-internal-quality-gates-internal-only-45"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 46,
        "heading": "stack-* Lineage (Tech Stack Reference) — REFERENCE",
        "startLine": 305,
        "endLine": 317,
        "content": "### stack-* Lineage (Tech Stack Reference) — REFERENCE\n\n```\nstack-* skills are shipped reference documentation.\nThey document specific technology stacks.\n\nstack-* tools:\n  read — allow\n  grep — allow\n  glob — allow\n  All others — ask (reference skills are read-only)\n```",
        "characterCount": 271,
        "estimatedTokens": 68,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#stack-lineage-tech-stack-reference-reference-46"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 47,
        "heading": "Unprefixed (Framework-Agnostic) — NEUTRAL",
        "startLine": 318,
        "endLine": 327,
        "content": "### Unprefixed (Framework-Agnostic) — NEUTRAL\n\n```\nopencode-config-workflow is framework-agnostic.\nTools determined by the OpenCode config workflow needs.\nTypically: read, glob, grep, configure-primitive.\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 213,
        "estimatedTokens": 54,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#unprefixed-framework-agnostic-neutral-47"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 48,
        "heading": "Part 4: Permission Pattern Reference",
        "startLine": 328,
        "endLine": 329,
        "content": "## Part 4: Permission Pattern Reference",
        "characterCount": 39,
        "estimatedTokens": 10,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#part-4-permission-pattern-reference-48"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 49,
        "heading": "Granular Bash Rules",
        "startLine": 330,
        "endLine": 343,
        "content": "### Granular Bash Rules\n\n```json\n{\n  \"bash\": {\n    \"*\": \"ask\",\n    \"git *\": \"allow\",\n    \"node *\": \"allow\",\n    \"npm *\": \"allow\",\n    \"npx *\": \"allow\"\n  }\n}\n```",
        "characterCount": 161,
        "estimatedTokens": 41,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#granular-bash-rules-49"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 50,
        "heading": "Granular Edit Rules (Path-Scoped)",
        "startLine": 344,
        "endLine": 355,
        "content": "### Granular Edit Rules (Path-Scoped)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"edit\": {\n    \"*\": \"ask\",\n    \".opencode/**\": \"allow\",\n    \"src/tools/**\": \"allow\"\n  }\n}\n```",
        "characterCount": 144,
        "estimatedTokens": 36,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#granular-edit-rules-path-scoped-50"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 51,
        "heading": "Granular Task Rules (Agent-Type-Scoped)",
        "startLine": 356,
        "endLine": 367,
        "content": "### Granular Task Rules (Agent-Type-Scoped)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"task\": {\n    \"*\": \"ask\",\n    \"hm-l2-reviewer\": \"allow\",\n    \"hm-l2-*\": \"allow\"\n  }\n}\n```",
        "characterCount": 147,
        "estimatedTokens": 37,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#granular-task-rules-agent-type-scoped-51"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 52,
        "heading": "Granular Skill Rules (Lineage-Scoped)",
        "startLine": 368,
        "endLine": 382,
        "content": "### Granular Skill Rules (Lineage-Scoped)\n\n```json\n{\n  \"skill\": {\n    \"*\": \"ask\",\n    \"hm-*\": \"allow\",\n    \"gate-*\": \"allow\",\n    \"stack-*\": \"allow\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 164,
        "estimatedTokens": 41,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#granular-skill-rules-lineage-scoped-52"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 53,
        "heading": "Part 5: 56-Agent Tool Allowance Summary",
        "startLine": 383,
        "endLine": 391,
        "content": "## Part 5: 56-Agent Tool Allowance Summary\n\nBased on actual agent definitions in `.opencode/agents/` (April 2026):\n\n| Depth | Count | Tool Pattern |\n|-------|-------|-------------|\n| **L0 Primary** | 2 (hm, hf) | read+glob+grep+task+skill; no edit/write/bash |\n| **L1 Coordinator** | 2 (hm, hf) | read+glob+grep+task(specific)+bash(git,node); no edit/write |\n| **L2 hm Specialist** | 44 | Domain-specific: read-only (researchers/reviewers) or read-write (executors/builders); bash restricted to git+node+npx |",
        "characterCount": 509,
        "estimatedTokens": 128,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#part-5-56-agent-tool-allowance-summary-53"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 54,
        "heading": "Part 5: 56-Agent Tool Allowance Summary",
        "startLine": 392,
        "endLine": 398,
        "content": "| **L2 hf Specialist** | 10 | read+edit/write(scoped to .opencode/)+bash(git,node)+glob+grep; task denied or restricted |\n| **GSD Agents** | 33 | Varies by role; gsd-executor has full read-write; gsd-code-reviewer is read-only |\n\n**Key observation:** 0 out of 56 agents have unrestricted `bash` permission. All bash access is pattern-restricted.\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 350,
        "estimatedTokens": 88,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#part-5-56-agent-tool-allowance-summary-54"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 55,
        "heading": "Anti-Patterns",
        "startLine": 399,
        "endLine": 405,
        "content": "## Anti-Patterns\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Detection | Correction |\n|-------------|-----------|------------|\n| **The Gate-Opener** — allowing unrestricted bash to L2 agents | `bash: allow` without patterns | Restrict to `git *`, `node *`, `npm *`, `npx *` |\n| **The Cross-Lineage Breaker** — hm agent loading hf skill | hm agent frontmatter includes hf-* in skills array | Remove hf-* skills; cross-lineage access is hf→hm only |\n| **The Delegation Leaker** — L2 agent allowed to delegate via task | `task: allow` on read-only agent | Set `task: \"*\": ask` for all L2 specialists |",
        "characterCount": 573,
        "estimatedTokens": 144,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#anti-patterns-55"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 56,
        "heading": "Anti-Patterns",
        "startLine": 406,
        "endLine": 411,
        "content": "| **The File-Scoper** — edit allowed on entire project | `edit: allow` without path restriction | Scope to specific directories: `.opencode/**`, `src/**` |\n| **The Harness Abuser** — L2 agent using delegate-task | `delegate-task: allow` on specialist agent | ask all harness delegation tools on L2 |\n| **The Blind Agent** — no read permission but expected to analyze code | `read: ask` on researcher agent | Always allow `read` on any agent expected to read code |\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 471,
        "estimatedTokens": 118,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#anti-patterns-56"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 57,
        "heading": "Self-Correction",
        "startLine": 412,
        "endLine": 413,
        "content": "## Self-Correction",
        "characterCount": 18,
        "estimatedTokens": 5,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#self-correction-57"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 58,
        "heading": "Mode 1: Permission Too Restrictive (Agent Cannot Function)",
        "startLine": 414,
        "endLine": 424,
        "content": "### Mode 1: Permission Too Restrictive (Agent Cannot Function)\n\n```\nIs the agent failing with \"permission denied\" errors?\n├── Check the tool name in the error → is it needed for the agent's domain?\n│   ├── YES → Add a granular allow rule scoped to the specific use case\n│   ├── NO → The agent is attempting unauthorized work → flag for review\n│   └── UNCLEAR → Check the agent's skills list and role description\n└── After adding permission → verify the agent can complete its task\n```",
        "characterCount": 484,
        "estimatedTokens": 121,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#mode-1-permission-too-restrictive-agent-cannot-function-58"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 59,
        "heading": "Mode 1: Permission Too Restrictive (Agent Cannot Function)",
        "startLine": 425,
        "endLine": 426,
        "content": "**Example:** Researcher agent getting `webfetch: ask` errors. If research-domain agent, add `webfetch: allow`. If execution-domain agent, ask is correct — the agent is straying from its domain.",
        "characterCount": 195,
        "estimatedTokens": 49,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#mode-1-permission-too-restrictive-agent-cannot-function-59"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 60,
        "heading": "Mode 2: Permission Too Permissive (Agent Has Unnecessary Access)",
        "startLine": 427,
        "endLine": 438,
        "content": "### Mode 2: Permission Too Permissive (Agent Has Unnecessary Access)\n\n```\nIs the agent using tools outside its domain?\n├── Audit successful tool calls → which tools are used?\n├── Check for unused tool permissions → list tools allowed but never called\n│   ├── UNUSED → Remove the permission (principle of least privilege)\n│   └── RARELY USED → Consider ask instead of allow\n└── Check for cross-lineage tool usage → hm agent using hf tools?\n    └── YES → Violation. Remove the tool and route the task.\n```",
        "characterCount": 503,
        "estimatedTokens": 126,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#mode-2-permission-too-permissive-agent-has-unnecessary-access-60"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 61,
        "heading": "Mode 3: Lineage Boundary Violation",
        "startLine": 439,
        "endLine": 449,
        "content": "### Mode 3: Lineage Boundary Violation\n\n```\nIs this an hm agent attempting meta-builder work?\n├── Agent tried to load hf-* skill → BLOCK. Route to hf-orchestrator.\n├── Agent tried to use configure-primitive → BLOCK. Route to hf-meta-builder.\n├── Agent tried to use validate-restart → BLOCK. Route to hf-meta-builder.\n├── Agent tried to delegate to hf-* agent → BLOCK. Cross-lineage delegation is hf→hm only.\n└── Agent is doing meta-concept work → Route user to hf lineage.\n```",
        "characterCount": 476,
        "estimatedTokens": 119,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#mode-3-lineage-boundary-violation-61"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 62,
        "heading": "Mode 4: MCP Tool Availability Confusion",
        "startLine": 450,
        "endLine": 462,
        "content": "### Mode 4: MCP Tool Availability Confusion\n\n```\nAgent expected MCP tool but it's not available?\n├── Check opencode.json for the MCP server configuration\n├── Check if the required API key is set (env var)\n│   ├── NOT SET → MCP tools from that provider are not available\n│   └── SET → Check MCP server is running; restart OpenCode if needed\n├── Check if the tool name matches the actual MCP naming convention\n│   └── MCP tools may use mcp__provider__tool or provider_tool format\n└── If tool is confirmed unavailable → redesign without it or add the provider\n```",
        "characterCount": 560,
        "estimatedTokens": 140,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#mode-4-mcp-tool-availability-confusion-62"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 63,
        "heading": "Maximum Correction Attempts",
        "startLine": 463,
        "endLine": 471,
        "content": "### Maximum Correction Attempts\n\n3 per permission configuration cycle. After 3 correction cycles without resolution:\n- Document the agent's domain and the specific permission gap\n- Report: `Agent: <name>, Blocked Tool: <tool>, Reason: <why it's needed>, Attempts: <count>`\n- Escalate to manual review with the matrix as context\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 332,
        "estimatedTokens": 83,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#maximum-correction-attempts-63"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 64,
        "heading": "Validation Loop",
        "startLine": 472,
        "endLine": 485,
        "content": "## Validation Loop\n\n```\ndo → audit → fix → repeat\n\n1. AUDIT: Read the target agent's frontmatter permissions\n2. COMPARE: Check each allowed tool against this matrix (depth + lineage rules)\n3. DETECT: Flag violations (cross-lineage, overly permissive, missing required)\n4. FIX: Apply granular rules following the templates in Section 4\n5. VERIFY: Confirm the agent can perform its domain tasks\n```\n\n---",
        "characterCount": 401,
        "estimatedTokens": 101,
        "id": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md#validation-loop-64"
      },
      {
        "path": ".opencode/skills/hm-l3-tool-capability-matrix/SKILL.md",
        "index": 65,
        "heading": "Cross-References",
        "startLine": 486,
        "endLine": 497,
```

```
        "content": "## Overview\n\nDefine and enforce bidirectional agent↔skill bindings across all 5 Hivemind lineages.         "content": "## Agent-to-Skill Bindings\n\nEach agent domain loads a mandatory set of skills. These are non-negotiable — an agent without its required skills is under-equipped for its task domain.",
```
